My all-time fave ingredients:

~ Benzoyl peroxide: kills acne-causing bacteria
~ Salicylic acid: unclog pores and reduces sebum production
~ Retinal: targets uneven skin texture, fine lines and wrinkles 
~ Niacinamide: regulates sebum production
~ Lactic acid: removes dead skin cells that could clog pores and cause acne

Fun fact, the choice between leave-on and rinse -off products with exfoliating acids depends on your skin type and personal preferences. 

Benefits of the rinse-off products (cleansers/masks):

🌟 Streamlines your skincare routine (fewer steps)
🌟 Less risk of irritation (especially when using another active ingredient in the same routine, e.g retinoids)
🌟 Some products are gentle enough for daily use 
🌟 Particularly great for individuals with sensitive skin

Benefits of the leave-on products(serums/toners/moisturisers):

🌟 Offers continuous treatment over an extended period of time
🌟 Great for managing widespread texture or larger areas of concern

A key factor to always consider is the concentration of the ingredient in the product. A cleanser with 10% glycolic acid might be more irritating than a serum containing 2% glycolic acid. 

Are you team leave-on or team rinse-off? I’m a simple girl, I use both.

Skincare advice I’d give my younger self:

1. Apply sunscreen everywhere (and everyday).
2. Do not pick your acne.
3. A hydrated skin is a happy skin.
4. Do not neglect your neck and chest.
5. Use antioxidants.
6. Go easy on chemical exfoliation.
7. Lifestyle factors affect your skin.
8. Ditch the makeup wipes and double cleanse instead.
9. Skincare can’t fix everything.
10. It pays to be an informed consumer.

Did I miss anything? What skincare tips do you wish you knew earlier?

Just here to show off one of my fave active cleansers!

This cleanser is formulated with 2% salicylic acid (BHA). I love using it when I don’t feel like using my leave-on BHA product. 

Depending on my skin needs, I typically use this 2-5 times a week. It targets comedonal acne (whiteheads / blackheads) and skin texture, meaning the cleanser is great for treating and preventing acne. 

The cleanser has a gel consistency, doesn’t strip my skin, and foams up nicely. I typically leave it on my skin for about five minutes before washing it off. Because this is a wash-off product, I’m able to use more actives in my routine.

I have zero complaints about this one! I highly recommend to all skin types, especially oily, acne-prone peeps 💕

I heard some of you do not apply sunscreen on your bodies. Why?🥹

The skin on your body is as important as the skin on your face. It’s even more important to apply sunscreen on your body now that the weather is warmer and we’re wearing fewer clothes. 

Here are some body sunscreen recommendations. My personal requirements for a body sunscreen are:

- must be affordable
- 150ml or above
- zero white cast
- spf 50 or above
- moisturising
- glowy finish
- can be repurchased in-store

On that note, my fave body sunscreen is the Hawaiian Tropic one, followed by the white Bondi Sands sunscreens. I usually buy Bondi Sands when Hawaiian Tropic is out of stock. 

PS: you can use one sunscreen for your face and body. I just prefer to use a different sunscreen on my body to save cost.

What sunscreen do you use on your body?

I love discovering new skincare brands! Let’s talk about some products from @smoengi.

🔅 Facial Foaming Cleanser: Contains hyaluronic acid, vitamin C, vitamin B5, chamomile extract. This is a gentle foaming cleanser with a citrusy fragrance. It’s hydrating, non-drying, and doesn’t irritate my skin. I love it and will be using it as my travel cleanser (because of the size).

🔅 Platinum Ageless Serum: Contains hyaluronic acid, peptides, buddleja davidii extract, vitamin C. This is a fab hydrating product with antioxidant properties. This creates a great base for my moisturiser to sink into my skin. The serum is slightly viscous and also contains fragrance. I prefer hydrating products that have a really viscous consistency so I usually pair this with my Hada Labo premium lotion when I have the time. The only issue with this product is the dispenser— it is a little stiff so the product usually splatters each time I use it. 

🔅 Signature Everlasting Youth Cream: Contains vitamin E, Coenzyme Q10, vitamin B5, centella asiatica. A super lightweight, hydrating moisturiser that easily fits into a morning routine for the warmer months. This is a no-fuss moisturiser with a delicious ingredient list. I just wish the moisturiser didn’t sit on my face before absorbing into the skin. I typically don’t wait in-between my routine steps so the slow absorption takes away from the experience.

I managed to gather some of my faves into one video. If you’re still trying to build a routine, you can start from here.

 ☀️ Cerave Acne Foaming Cleanser: contains 4% benzoyl peroxide. Incredible gentle and gets rid of mild - moderate inflamed acne within a few days.

☀️ Isntree Hyaluronic Acid Watery Sun Gel: zero white cast, moisturising, and has an elegant finish. Sits well under makeup and great for reapplication.

And that’s it! Have you tried any of these products?

Let me tell you about the @sachiskin Ursolic Acid & Retinal serum, A.K.A my fave multi-functional product.

For the newbies here, retinoids are one of the most effective ingredients for treating multiple skin concerns. Retinal is a type of retinoid.

This product offers all the benefits of retinal without any negative effects, thanks to the gentle formulation. It reduces hyperpigmentation and minimises the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Retinal promotes collagen production and skin cell turnover, so this product helps me to achieve that glow-from-within skin. 

I also deal with less acne now because retinal has antibacterial properties, meaning it eliminates acne-causing bacteria. Retinal also helps to reduce oil production, which is great for preventing breakouts. So you see, this one product offers several benefits to the skin. 

It’s been an absolute pleasure using this product. I like to describe it as the icing on the cake. My skin was doing well before I incorporated this product into my routine, but this took my skin to the next level. My fave noticeable difference is how my skin always looks vibrant 🤩.

I really recommend this to all skin types. It’s a great investment.

Before you buy this, please note that I had an established routine before using this product, and it took several monthsss to see significant results.

🩹✨ Bye-bye, scars! 👋 ✨

Struggling with acne scars or other blemishes? I got you! Here’s my tried-and-true routine for smoother, clearer skin:

1️⃣ Topical Retinoid: This powerhouse ingredient speeds up cell turnover, fading scars and promoting collagen production. Some great options to consider are:

The Ordinary Granactive Retinoid 2% Emulsion: A gentle yet effective retinoid for beginners.
CeraVe Resurfacing Retinol Serum: A great option for sensitive skin, formulated with encapsulated retinol for gradual release.
Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%: An over-the-counter retinoid that’s been proven to treat acne and improve the appearance of scars.
(Remember, always consult your dermatologist for the right retinoid for your skin type!)

2️⃣ Cicaplast: This soothing balm accelerates healing and repairs the skin barrier, perfect for post-acne inflammation.
3️⃣ Silicone Sheets: These create an optimal healing environment, reducing scar redness, thickness, and itching.

Remember, consistency is key! Stick with this routine for a few months to see the best results. 💖

#scarremoval #acnescars #skincaretips #skingoals #healthyskin #glowingskin #siliconesheets #keloidscars #hyperpigmentation #pigmentedscars #darkscars

✨Shaving often causes: razor burn, ingrown hairs and hyperpigmentation, here are some tips for a smooth and soothed bikini line ✨

1️⃣ Salicylic Acid Body Wash: Your secret weapon against ingrown hairs! Gently exfoliates and unclogs pores.
2️⃣ Bikini Line-friendly Razor: If you must shave, opt for a razor specifically designed for the bikini area.
3️⃣ Regular Blade Changes: Dull blades cause irritation and cuts. Switch out your blade regularly.
4️⃣ Glycolic Acid Body Balm: This powerhouse ingredient fades hyperpigmentation over time.
5️⃣ Itch Relief Cream: Soothes razor burn instantly with aloe vera or hydrocortisone.
6️⃣ Benzoyl Peroxide Gel: Calms inflammation from ingrown hairs and fights bacteria.

This conversation needs to be had! Age isn’t your Que to start Botox! It’s never a determinant. The sole determinant is how you feel about the wrinkles and expression lines on your face. 

Let me also add that there is nothing wrong with expression lines, it’s a sign that you are living. A sign that you are laughing. 

Getting Fillers and Botox are expensive (to most people) it’s not a one off thing, you need to make sure it actually fits your economic realities. (You can always quit if it longer 😃)

Lastly, I beg you in the name of all that you hold dear to see only qualified injectors. Doctors and Aesthetic Nurses. 

It’s not a trend to jump on. It should be on a needs met basis. 

Have a great week ahead 🥰

#theskinpriest #skincare

I’m talking about the day to day BS. At this time in life, when there is way less time ahead then there is behind, I seem to have developed a radar for BS and a lack of tolerance for it. Sometimes I call it out, but mostly I just keep it moving and go find my joy. What do you do?

58 AND TRULY LIVING; “Wait! What! It’s July?!! The first half of 2024 is done already?! And what an incredible 6 months it’s been. I’ve had to contend with the pain and immobility caused by having a ‘frozen shoulder’, which is still ongoing. At times it felt as if this condition, was holding me back, but when I went through my camera roll I realised that I really did push through and LIVE regardless!! In my 58 years I’ve learned the importance of a balanced life. For me that looks like enjoying your work, family and solo time, but also intentionally welcoming positive people with good energy into my life.. and I think it shows in these videos. My tribe is one I’m so happy to be a part of, we instinctively uplift one another… I’m so grateful for the connections I’ve made, new and old, in real life and on The Gram. You encourage and inspire me daily… thank you for filling the past 6 months with your good vibes! 🙏🏽 🤍
.

I’ve been asked this question a few times recently… “What advice would you give to someone struggling with getting older?” A good way to switch your mindset and to kick start the trajectory to thrive in every season is to view each year as a level.  With each birthday we just get better and better at this thing called life. It’s like levelling up! Every birthday should be greeted with a celebration of accomplishment. There is even growth in the difficult times.. you survived! You learnt something! Last year I fractured my ankle, and from the pain and immobility I learned that it’s ok to ask for help when you feel vulnerable… Something this strong, fiercely independent woman has always found difficult. I also learned not to take mobility for granted. So there was definitely growth and personal development even in that debilitating experience. And whilst I think it’s important to reflect and identify your accomplishments looking to the next level is important too. It’s exciting to look at the year ahead. It’ll be filled with opportunity and you can plan on further personal development and taking things to the next level!!
.
.
.
#birthdays #miamaugé #growingold #silverhair #levellingup #midlife #over50 #over40 #lifelessons #growth #positivethinking #positiveageing

On Father’s Day. Postcards from my father… sent to me when I was around 3 or 4 years old, he was touring in Europe as a drummer and I always missed him when he was away… these postcards, some photos and fond memories of his love is all I have today. Doing my best to be grateful for the short time I had with him, but I always shed a tear on Father’s Day. If the “step” in stepparent stands for “step up” he most definitely did that!! From the age of one he showed me pure parental love. He was my safe place, my happy place… he was my hero.. he was simply my dad 🙏🏽🤍 
.
Thank you to @themightysparrow_sf for the music that helps to keep my memories alive 🇹🇹
.

AD - Retinol and retinal, both vitamin A derivatives, are essential in skincare but differ in potency and application.
Retinol, widely available over-the-counter, boosts cell turnover and collagen production, reducing fine lines and improving skin texture. It’s less potent and requires conversion to retinal and then to retinoic acid, making it gentler and suitable for sensitive skin or retinoid beginners.

Retinal, or retinaldehyde, is more potent and one step closer to retinoic acid. It offers faster results for acne and photoaging but can be more irritating. It’s ideal for those seeking significant skin improvements.

Medik8’s Crystal Retinal system makes it very easy to introduce an effective Retinal into your regime. They have a ladder and so you can start at the bottom, or at higher strengths if you are a seasoned retinoid user.  Either way, you can feel reassured that you are using a high quality product that will deliver results without compromising your skin health!  @officialmedik8

What’s in a name? I have spent half my life explaining how to pronounce my name to people. I don’t mind doing it these days, but as a child it caused me such anxiety when a new teacher, doing registration, who hadn’t realised the importance of getting a pupil’s name right would carelessly call out “Maya Mange” or “Magoo”, Morgay, or even “Morgue”.. and the classroom would ripple with suppressed infantile tittering. It crushed little me. Today I’m happy with my name, but back then I remember telling my mum I was going to legally change my name to Tina as soon as I was 18. When you’re young you just want to fit in. This audio reminded me of this, and made me curious. Do you think I look like a Tina, a Mia or something else? Let me know? #justforfun

Dhokla Idli Recipe

- 1 cup besan (gram flour)
- 1/2 cup semolina (rava)
- 1/2 cup yogurt (dahi)
- 1/2 cup water (adjust as needed)
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp Eno fruit salt or baking soda
- 1 tsp ginger-green chili paste
- 1 tsp sugar
- Salt to taste

For Tempering:
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp sesame seeds
- 1-2 green chilies, slit
- A few curry leaves
- A pinch of asafoetida (hing)
- 1/4 cup water
- 1 tsp sugar

1. Prepare the Batter: In a mixing bowl, combine besan, semolina, yogurt, water, lemon juice, ginger-green chili paste, sugar, and salt. Mix well to form a smooth batter. Adjust the consistency with more water if needed. The batter should be thick but pourable. Let the batter rest for 15-20 minutes.

2. Steam the Idlis :Grease idli moulds with a little oil. Just before steaming, add Eno fruit salt or baking soda to the batter. Mix gently and immediately pour the batter into the idli moulds. Steam the idlis in an idli steamer or pressure cooker (without the whistle) for about 10-12 minutes or until a toothpick inserted comes out clean.

3. Prepare the Tempering: In a small pan, heat oil. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add sesame seeds, slit green chilies, curry leaves, and asafoetida. Sauté for a few seconds. Add 1/4 cup of water and 1 tsp of sugar. Bring it to a boil and then turn off the heat.

4. Once the idlis are done, remove them from the moulds and place them on a serving plate. Pour the prepared tempering over the idlis, ensuring they are well-coated.

5. Garnish and Enjoy: Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves and freshly grated coconut, serve hot with green chutney or tamarind chutney.

Sri Lankan Inspired Coconut Chicken Curry with Ragi idli

- 1.5 lbs (700g) chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
- 1 can (400ml) coconut milk
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil or coconut oil
- 1 large onion, finely chopped
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1-inch piece of ginger, grated
- 2 -3 large ripe tomatoes, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp tomato purée 
- 2-3 green chillies, slit lengthwise (adjust to taste)
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp ground coriander
- 1 tsp fennel seeds
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp fenugreek seeds
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 2-3 cloves
- 4-5 curry leaves
- 1 tsp paprika
- 1 tsp ground black pepper
- 1 tbsp tamarind paste or 2 tbsp lemon juice
- Salt to taste
- Fresh coriander, chopped (for garnish)
- Lemon wedges (for serving)

1. Season the chicken pieces with a bit of salt and set aside.

2. In a large pot or deep pan, heat the vegetable oil or coconut oil over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, cinnamon stick, and cloves. Sauté until the seeds start to splutter.

3. Add the chopped onion and sauté until golden brown. Then add the minced garlic, grated ginger, and curry leaves. Sauté for another minute until fragrant.

4. Add the turmeric powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, and ground black pepper. Stir well to coat the onions. Add the chopped tomatoes and puree and cook until they soften and the oil starts to separate from the mixture.

5. Add the chicken pieces to the pot, stirring well to coat them with the spice mixture. Cook the chicken until it starts to brown.

6. Add the coconut milk, green chillies, and tamarind paste (or lemon juice) to the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot and let the curry simmer for about 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender. Stir occasionally 

7. Season the curry with salt to taste. If the curry is too thick, you can add a little water to reach the desired consistency. Garnish with fresh coriander.

Enjoy!

#curry #indianfood #healthyeating

Daal with Coconut Cream Vegetables

- 1 cup split red lentils
- 1 can (160ml) coconut cream
- 2 cups vegetable broth or water
- 1 cup mixed frozen vegetables (e.g., peas, carrots, corn, and green beans)
- 1 medium onion, finely chopped
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1-inch piece of ginger, grated
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil or ghee
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp ground coriander
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1/2 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
- Salt to taste
- squeeze of lemon juice

1. Rinse the split red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.

2. n a large pot, heat the vegetable oil or ghee over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and mustard seeds. Once they start to splutter, add the chopped onion. Sauté until the onion becomes golden brown.

3. Add the minced garlic and grated ginger. Sauté for another minute until fragrant.

4. Add the turmeric powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, red chili powder, and garam masala. Stir well and cook for about 1 minute. 

5. Add the rinsed lentils to the pot and stir to coat them with the spice mixture. Pour in the vegetable broth or water and bring the mixture to a boil.

6. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the lentils simmer for about 15 minutes, or until they are soft and fully cooked. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

7. Once the lentils are cooked, add the coconut cream and mixed frozen vegetables. Stir well and let the daal simmer for another 5-10 minutes, or until the vegetables are heated through and tender. Adjust the consistency with more broth or water if needed.

8. I made a quick tadka to add an extra layer of flavour - heat 2tbsp of ghee in a small pan, and add 1 tbsp of chopped garlic a pinch of cumin seeds, mustards seeds and a large pinch of asoefitida, let this sizzle, if you like it spicy add some chopped green chillis to this tadka, once its all a light brown colour add this ghee to the pot of daal. Give the daal a squeeze of lemon, and give it taste before serving.
9. Enjoy hot with topping of crispy paneer croutons, like me :)

The BINDI ⚫️ 

Can we have a bindi that tells us when it’s time to top up our spf?

Ancient origins of the bindi date back to at least the 3rd century. Signifying so many things, marital status, wisdom - the third eye, and even spiritual insights. 
Historically the ‘dot’ could be made from natural ingredients like turmeric, sandalwood, and vermillion and was perhaps even believed to protect against evil spirits and negative energies. 

I have always had a pack of bindis in my drawer, probably since I was a toddler. Placing the bindi between my brows is an inherent habit that to this day, makes me feel instantly ‘different’. It probably speaks to the dual sided life I have always led: the Indian and the British… with so much crossover in between.

Nowadays, a fashion accessory, it will always mean so much more to me… I think it looks pretty good with my white shirt today… no?

Happy Friday lovelies, hope you have a fantastic weekend ahead xxx

I truly believe ‘kitchen time’ is directly proportional to good health and happiness, don’t you think? 

I don’t think my mum was trying to actively discourage me, but I do see why some of the women of that generation might have actively discouraged their daughters from being in the kitchen. 

This isn’t really about girl or boy, it’s about creating a central place in the house where everyone gathers to eat at some point in the evening, and everyone should be involved in the prep and cooking and cleaning 😊 before or after whatever other chore they need to do 😅

We have really been enjoying the Ragi idlis recently, they’re so quick and easy and pair so well with curry 😍 have you tried?

Sometimes kids are the best teachers… and they’re great at reminding you of your age! But my kids know I love a challenge … and I obviously still have a long way to go!

It wasn’t until around 2021 that I started to notice what I could no longer do, and improving my joint flexibility became a priority.

I began practicing yoga and Pilates daily (well, most days lol) and the aches in my lower back got so much better. My posture and overall muscle tone improved, and my core strength—so hard to regain after having kids—significantly increased.

To create new neural pathways, we need to embrace change and take on new challenges. Without change, there’s no improvement.

I’m not motivated to work out every day, but it’s become a habit for me now. Even when motivation wanes, habit takes over.

Do you practice yoga or Pilates? If so, how and where do you do it?

A peek into my personal philosophy as a professional for 14 years...

After graduating with my esthetics license, I was determined to explore as much as possible about both the medical and holistic aspects of the beauty industry. I pursued advanced certifications in microdermabrasion, chemical peels, and dermaplaning, along with specialized training in hyperpigmentation, acne, reflexology, aromatherapy, herbal medicine, sound therapy, and lymphatic drainage. 

I believe estheticians are 𝒐𝒓𝒄𝒉𝒆𝒔𝒕𝒓𝒂𝒕𝒐𝒓𝒔 𝒐𝒇 𝒆𝒏𝒆𝒓𝒈𝒚. There’s so much power in touch. This belief formed the basis for my signature treatments that I now offer at my private studio. I am incredibly passionate about my craft and have made it my mission to inspire other practitioners through a new approach to esthetics. My trademarked technique combines the best of Eastern and Western medicine teachings to create an experience for the skin, mind, and senses. I firmly believe that you shouldn’t have to sacrifice relaxation for results in the treatment room.

✨ A question… is it time for you? 

Are you sick of the dyeing, the irritation, the cost, the time wasted and the huge pressure to keep those silver strands hidden? 

I have to say to you, right now, if you’re scared to transition but desperate, that the end is worth every second of the journey. 

So go for it - do it for you and for your health (hair dye is nasty stuff) - stay strong and keep going and I promise you’ll be different, really different by the time you chop off those last dry old ends. 

Ask away if you need advice, and don’t forget to join the #silversisters community for extra support - they’re AMAZING! 

I’ve never regretted my decision to stop dyeing and never will. 

I hope to see you on the silver side, and good luck! 🤍✨🤍✨

✨The amazing @outernetglobal Emoji Shower at Tottenham Court Road reminded me how experiencing cancer felt throughout, and still does at times. I’m sure it’s the same for everyone, and it was a profound and life changing experience - particularly the second time. 

Here are some things I’ve learnt during my recovery and quest to reduce the chance of it happening again.

1. It’s short, this life thing. Do the things that are important to you - even if they’re out of your comfort zone. Just do them. 

2. Cut down on meat and dairy. A plant based diet is preferable as studies show a higher risk of cancer recurrence if you eat meat and dairy.

3. Get flipping moving! Exercise is key to helping regulate hormones in the body so get the heck off your chair, Julie!

4. Ditch the booze. Alcohol raises your oestrogen levels and for me that is a negative so I drink very little. 

5. Go deep and meditate - dealing with breast cancer twice takes a mental toll and meditation has been instrumental in helping me support my mental health and keep stress at bay. 
I use the @calm app which is brilliant. 

6. Don’t keep it to yourself - talking about your cancer is key. I didn’t speak about the first cancer in 2001. I had no counselling and packed it away in a place inside that ultimately showed itself in other ways. Online support, friends, family and counselling for the second breast cancer made all the difference. I would recommend anyone to have it, if available. 

7. Finally, I look for the light and try to stay as positive as possible. I have the joy of still being here 🙏🤍✨

If any of these have resonated with you and you would like more information, please DM me with any questions otherwise thank you so much for following me. Please see @breastcancernow for up to date advice and how to reduce your risk of breast cancer. 

Much love 
Julie xx 

#breastcancer #breastcancerawareness #breastcancerreconstruction #emojishower #proage

Revamping my content from now on and I hope you’re excited about it too as much as I am!🩵 

Things I used to be obsessively curious about:

- Skincare ingredients
- Newest products
- Helping my audience and #PalmPalmFam to help achieve healthy skin

Which led me to focus on content that helps you buy the best skincare products for you in the past 7 years. And as a beauty content creator, I used to be on the frontline of #hyperconsumerism, churning out recommendations one after another. But lately, something’s changed. Witnessing myself and others relentlessly push “buy, buy, buy” became exhausting. My own “holy grail” products haven’t changed in years, and as I embraced mindful living, the dissonance became undeniable. This inspired me to create this, a guide towards a “buy less, buy slow, and buy better” approach for myself but also to share with anyone who’s interested in shifting their #consumption habits. 

We’re not here to thrive for perfection, but rather more imperfect actions collectively🌴

🎨Design by @kathyrinele

What if the beauty industry use more waste products? 

🍃Essential oils in your favorite beauty product & fragrance can be wasteful and unehtically sourced. 

🍋It requires 1,500 lemons to make 1 pound of lemon essential oil, and it takes around 10,000 pounds of rose petals 🌹to produce just one pound of rose essential oil.

🌳The demand for more natural ingredients are driving overharvesting and deforestation 

🌾if you use all natural ingredients, but land availability and ingredients themselves (i.e. roses) are scarce, then you may contribute to intensive land-use that could alternatively create food for people

So yes. Natural doesn’t mean that it’s more sustainable. And often times, the reality can be far dffirent from what we as custoers are imagininig. These are things that I’m learning more and more of recently by meeting farmers, talking to chefs, and researching the natural ingredients farm to production process. And it’s been encouraging us at @kravebeauty to source more responsibly.

I visited a strawberry farm in Nonsan, South Korea a few weeks back. and I learned that a small strawberry farm can produce 5 tons of leaves and stems waste. This is more than what a small farm can compost and small farms are struggling to pay the disposal fee for these organic waste. That made me realize that maybe the beauty industry should move away from using the whole fruits but instead try incorporating more agricultural byproducts.

🍓Then there are companies like @cocoberry__official who transforms farm waste into what’s good for your skin. 

🍷There’s a brand like @vinoir_official who upcycles wine pomace into beautiful products. 

🍇@kravebeauty’s Makeup Re-Wined also uses grapesed oil extracted from the grapeseeds that were left over from the wine industry

So what are your thoughts? Should the beauty industry move away from farming, and instead taking the farm waste?

Micro-needling was a procedure I had only a couple of times, loved the effects, and would only trust in a clinic via professionals with a lot of numbing cream…until now. I love an at-home treatment but was never remotely interested in derma-rolling, however @qureskincare Micro Infusion System had me curious and excited to try micro-stamping, a far more controlled version of micro-needling that seems fool-proof and safer for self-administration. #AD

The needles (24k gold-plated titanium surgical grade) are 0.5mm which is the minimal length required to achieve results. This will penetrate the top layer of the skin. They are thinner than hair with less discomfort and very short downtime. The redness and prickly pain lasted for about 20mins. By the next morning, my skin felt great.

There are 2 serums which I alternate and that feel luxurious. I have a definite preference for the Rejuvenation Serum with EGF. This uses SHoligopeptide1 which is a skin identical synthetic peptide with 53 amino acids and helps with reducing the appearance of lines and wrinkles and can brighten the look of dull skin. This is what’s used when I get a professional treatment.

The Hydra Soothing Serum is a great compliment with beta glucan, niacinamide, glycerin and sodium hyaluronate to deeply hydrates and plumps the skin to reduce the look of fine lines. Sometimes the leftover serum after treatment can last a few applications so I save and use over the next couple of days.

I’m using this every 3 weeks and love the immediate results aswell as what this can do longterm to build collagen and elasticity!

Can order via link in bio, Shop My Posts. Use code : GOTHAMISTA for 10% off!

How I use :
•  On clean skin and repeating each stroke at least 5x
•  Starting from décolleté I roll upwards towards the collarbone
•  Neck: I roll both ways. Upwards for lifting, downwards for drainage
•  Along the jawbone: I put more pressure while rolling from chin outwards to ear.
•  My favorite part is rolling from ear to ear, across cheeks and lips.
•  Along cheekbone to temple. I like to give my crow’s feet area extra attention.
•  Between the brows
•  Forehead rolling from temple to temple
•  Follow up with the Sublime Skin Intensive Serum.

After 28 days -25% wrinkle depth
*independent laboratory, instrumental evaluation, 40 women, 28 days of use

AD with @eucerinsverige 🤍I guess this is what I would have told myself, but it’s also what I would say to anyone struggling with their skin right now:

That shame that you might feel, that isn’t yours to carry. You might carry it nevertheless, but ultimately it is in no way a reflection of your worth, capabilities, or who you are as a person. You are not the hardships that you’re facing while struggling with your skin.

I know it’s easy to isolate, to want to hide from the world until your skin is in a better place, but you’re only making it more difficult to get through. You need to confide in people, learn to lean on others, and trust that it gets easier to get through with a little help from your friends.

All that money and energy that you’re spending on skincare? Turn that towards something else. All you need is a good cleanser, a moisturizer for your skin type, and an SPF and you’re fine. And that brand that you’ve been using since you were a kid is your go-to now (hint hint @eucerinsverige)

I know you think it’s a good idea to restrict your diet, but with your already complicated relationship with food, it’ll do more harm than good both on your mental health and also when it comes to your skin.

Take a hard look at the people you follow on social media. Do yourself a favor and unfollow people that make you feel bad about yourself. And always remember that the images that you see while scrolling are often edited and/or carefully curated images that don’t reflect reality.

Last but not least, keep doing and showing up to the things that you love. I know having issues with your skin makes it feel impossible to leave the house sometimes, but try. You need any boost of things or people that bring you joy. And you deserve to keep living your life to the fullest, no matter what you’re currently going through with your skin❤️‍🩹 #eucerinsverige

The way I see it is that I haven’t met ‘The One’ yet, and for me there are two types of ‘The One’. There’s ‘The One’ that comes ready made, just for you. You both just click, everything aligns and you soar together! That’s the dream… and I’m hopeful, but I’m very aware that this is rare. Then there’s ’The One’ that comes into your life, you connect, you understand them, and they understand you…. You ‘see’ each other, and although it’s not plain sailing, over time you become invested enough to want to make your love survive. No matter what… you are willing to put in the work. This is a beautiful kind of union, but at this time in life I’m finding it difficult to partake... to get to the point where I’m invested. I’m busy enjoying my life too much to open the doors to something that could potentially take a lot of ‘work’. Granted there are rewards for this kind of love, but right now, I just want it to flow or I gotta go! There is one additional option, and that’s to ‘settle’. To find someone that simply fills a void and ‘settle’ with them for the companionship. This option is most definitely not for me! So when people ask, and I get asked a lot, “How come you’re single?” I say, “By choice”. Of course there are moments of loneliness, and even exasperation, like “When is he gonna show up?!”, but there is something peaceful about being single.. there’s a freedom. Your time is yours to do with whatever you choose, whenever you choose. You are unattached and there is something really liberating about that. Empowering even. So for the most part I’m OK rolling solo…. For now. 
.
What are your thoughts?
.

Well I’m not really sitting back and waiting. Myself and so many other women like me show up on IG to hopefully make societal change. So to every young woman that sees this post… please know your worth and your value are not entirely tied up in your youth, external or otherwise. The sooner you know this the better prepared you’ll be for what’s to come. If you know that kindness and compassion should be nurtured and cultivated, and that the pursuit of wisdom is far more important than the fruitless  pursuit of the mythical fountain of youth. If you know that these are the things that stand the test of time and exude true beauty, then when your youth begins to visibly fade you won’t feel a devastating sense of loss, instead you will have the right mindset for what I call the ‘power years’. My 50s have been the years when I believe I truly bloomed. Came into my own. It would have been sooner had I not spent my 40s invested in limiting beliefs imposed on me by society. So for now, make connections and memories; a gift your future self will cherish, and remember that she will be the best version of you that you could ever imagine! 
.
.
.
.

I hear a lot from women who are struggling as they approach midlife. Trust me I get it. My 40s were spent fretting about how to position myself in a youth focussed society. Then I hit 50 and I said “No more!” I quit the negative talk and I began to focus on the positives. Here are 5 things I can share from my own experience, that I hope will help anyone in a midlife funk. 
1. More financial freedom. In my experience, after years of being a single mum, with my income spread across 3 people’s needs, I welcomed my daughter’s financial independence. It meant I had more to spend on myself with no mum guilt. Finally!!
2. A surge of confidence I’d never felt before. Knowing that I have less time ahead of me than behind me, I felt the urge to say “yes” more. Not to be reckless, but to take a chance on myself. Modelling came along when I was 54, I said yes, after many years of struggling with imposter syndrome. It was a leap out of my comfort zone, but that leap was the first step to a new life. I think we naturally push through any youthful insecurities to intentionally live out our best years. This attitude coupled with wisdom from years of experience is unstoppable! 
3. My daughter’s’ independence also signalled more time for me. Time for self care like I’ve never had before! Time to travel, to learn new skills! It also meant I could take more risks with my career too, when I needed to leave the day job to become a model and content creator I didn’t hesitate. I knew I could throw myself into it wholeheartedly! 
4. Chances are you may have lost close friends along the way (2 of mine passed in their 40s), so I have a heightened sense of how fragile and precious life is. It’s not to be taken for granted, so I want to make the most of what I have left. 
5. The joy of having adult children. I was so caught up in thinking about losing my ‘babies’, I didn’t know how much I was going to enjoy seeing my girls triumphs and accomplishments, how much I’d love being around them and having adult conversations… and shared experiences. They are my favourite travel companions and faithful cheerleaders. It’s so true… what you put in you get back tenfold!!

Anyone else? 

I feel it’s sometimes so hard to see that you’re literally on the brink of a burn out or at the point where the balance is tipping. That wheel keeps turning and it’s not until you stop, reflect and identify the issues, and make changes that could make things better for you. Those changes aren’t always possible either. Which makes it even harder. 

You would think at my age I would be better at getting that work life balance right, but trust me, still learning. I have an awful habit of running even faster on that hamster wheel when I get stressed! I know you guys probably think I have it all sorted at all times, but trust me, it’s far from. 

I guess I’m using this as a reminder to everyone on here to check in with themselves and make sure you can identify those aspects of your life that perhaps could be changed to make things better for you… take the time to do a little review xxx

Things that help ground me, is spending more time with friends and family and really recognising the things that make me truly happy…trying to focus on the things that really matter. The bigger picture. Practicing gratitude…

Easier said than done! 

If you have any tips on preventing burn out, do drop them below ⬇️

I have a family history of dementia and I am trying to do everything I can to reduce my risk of getting it. 

Engaging in regular physical exercise can significantly reduce the risk of developing dementia. Research suggests that regular physical activity can decrease the risk of dementia by up to 30-40%. 

Aerobic exercises, strength training, and even activities like walking have been associated with better cognitive health and a reduced risk of cognitive decline.

I now do cardio x 2 per week and weight training /strength training & yoga for the other 3 times

I actually really enjoy exercising now. How about you?

Masala Potato Salad

500g baby potatoes quartered into wedges
1/2 tsp turmeric powder
1/2 tsp coriander powder
1/2 tsp cumin powder (with a little whole cumin)
1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder (or less)
1/2 tsp garam masala 
Cooking oil 
Salt and pepper to taste

1. Preheat over/ airfryer to 200c Fan
2. Mix together the above ingredients and spread on an oven proof dish. Make sure everything is coated just right
3. Cook until golden and crispy on outside and cooked through (fork to check)
4. Remove from oven and let them cool completely.

1 cup Greek yoghurt
Large handful of chopped sprain onions
Half a handful of chopped dill
1/2 tsp chaat masala 

Add the above ingredients to the cooled potatoes, and mix well… 

Enjoy immediately or pop in the fridge for later. 

Ps. If you like a really soft potato in your salad, you can boil and then roast, for that extra soft fluffy centre and crispy edges but I am all about saving time here

Instant Ragi Dosa

1 cup ragi flour (finger millet flour)
1/2 cup rice flour
1/4 cup semolina (rava)
1/2 cup yogurt 

- Salt to taste
- Water, as needed
- Oil, for cooking

1. Take a large mixing bowl, combine ragi flour, rice flour, semolina, and yogurt. Mix well 
 2. Add water gradually to make a smooth batter with a pouring consistency, similar to that of regular dosa batter.
 Let the batter rest for about 15-20 minutes.

2. Heat a non-stick or cast-iron tawa (griddle) over medium heat. Once the tawa is hot, pour a ladleful of batter onto the center and spread it evenly in a circular motion to form a thin dosa. Drizzle a little oil around the edges of the dosa. Cook until the edges start to lift and the bottom is golden brown and crisp. Flip the dosa and cook for another minute or so on the other side.

Serve hot with your fav curry or chutney

When treating hyperpigmentation, I prefer to use a product that contains a cocktail of anti-pigmentation ingredients. This means I don’t have to combine several single ingredient products!

If that sounds like you, I’ve put together some fabulous products you might want to try. I don’t own all the products in the picture (most of them belong to my siblings ☺️).

Products featured (L-R):

~ @nichebeautylab PIH Sun Spot Fading Treatment: Tranexamic acid, kojic acid, azelaic acid derivative, niacinamide. This is a PR product and I can’t wait to try it!

~ @sachiskin Triphala Pigmentation Corrector: 3 brightening super fruits, hexylresorcinol, acetyl rheum rhaponticum root extract, silymarin. I’m currently using this and it’s been an absolute pleasure! I’ve purchased it twice.

~ @facetheory Exaglow Serum: Tranexamic acid, vitamin C, licorice, lactic acid, niacinamide. My brother uses this and it works for him. He’s purchased it about 3/4 times.

~ @caudalie Vinoperfect Dark Spot Correction Serum: Contains vineferine, which is a grapevine sap that provides an exceptional dark spot correcting action that is 62 times more effective than vitamin C. Seems like the vineferine does all the magic. This is my elder sister’s star hyperpigmentation product. She’s on her 4th bottle. 

~ @deviantskincare Pigmentation Corrector Serum: Azelaic acid derivative, diglucosyl gallic acid, niacinamide, hexylresorcinol, licorice, acetyl glycyl beta-alanine. This is another PR product. Will be trying it soon!

~ @gardenofwisdomskincare Alpha Arbutin 2% and Kojic Acid 1% Serum: Vitamin C derivative, kojic acid, alpha arbutin, niacinamide, paper mulberry extract. A fabulous ingredient list for the price if you ask me. This is the only hyperpigmentation product my younger sister uses. She has sensitive skin and is very picky. She’s purchased it three times.

And that’s it! As always, to achieve maximum results, make sure to wear sunscreen and also use ingredients that increase cellular turnover (AHAs, retinoids).

Hi guyss, I tried the Augustinus Bader Cream Mask last weekend and was pleased with the results.

The mask is fragrance-free and formulated with the brand’s patented TFC8 technology, which features various hydrating and de-pigmentation ingredients, including polysaccharide complex, vitamin C, hyaluronic acid, ginseng extract, pomegranate extract and Chinese roots extract. 

I didn’t have huge expectations for this mask because as you can see in the video, my skin looked very healthy before I applied it. Buttt, I must give credit where it is due. The mask delivered instant hydration and revealed smoother, brighter skin (especially from the following day onwards). When I woke up the next day, I definitely noticed a healthy glow which wasn’t there before. I did not experience any dryness or skin irritations.

The brands says the mask can also be worn overnight, but I prefer not to. Ultimately, if you have some coins to spare, this mask could work for all skin types. Just make sure you manage your expectations. 

I personally will be using this as an alternative to my lactic acid serum because both products offer similar benefits (i.e smoother, brighter skin + enhances other products’ penetration).

SOME KEY INGREDIENTS 
~  Triphala: a mix of three super fruits which helps to fight free radicals and fade hyperpigmentation 

~ Acetyl Glycyl Beta Alanine: a peptide that prevents localised areas of pigment from appearing on the skin

~ Acetyl Rheum Rhaponticum Root Extract and Hexylresorcinol: advanced skin brighteners

~ Glycerin & bisabolol: skin soothers and reparatives

~ Phyllanthus emblica: antioxidant rich in vitamin C

~ Diglucosyl Gallic Acid: has powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties 

TEXTURE
This has a light lotion texture that feels nourishing on the skin. It pairs well with my other skin products and doesn’t pill. It easily glides over the skin and absorbs almost immediately. 

MY EXPERIENCE 
I am on my second bottle and honestly, I’m a very happy girl rn. This product is so gentle and applies on my skin without the slightest irritation. I’ve been using it 1-2 times daily for just over a month now. My dark spots are gradually fading and I know they’ll be fully gone within the next few weeks. Of course, I pair this with other key products like my retinal and AHA serum to get the best results.

FINAL THOUGHTS
This is an all round hyperpigmentation product - it targets multiple steps in the pigment pathway. It’s on the pricey end but I think it’s a great investment. The process of fading hyperpigmentation can take weeks - months so note that you’ll most likely have to use more than one bottle to achieve the best results. Long story cut short, this is a great product yall.

Okayy, get in here if you’re dealing with textured skin. Skin texture can include bumpy skin, enlarged pores, and comedonal acne (think blackheads and whiteheads). If that sounds like something you’re dealing with, salicylic acid might just be what you need.

Salicylic acid (BHA) gets rid of skin texture by travelling deep into the pores and removing all the gunk, leading to a smoother, even skin texture. Now that we’ve gotten that out of the way, let’s discuss these two cleansers. 

For starters, what differentiates both cleansers? Spoiler alert — nothing much.

Both cleansers:

- Contain 2% BHA
- Do not contain fragrance
- Have a gel texture that lathers up nicely
- Have a gentle formula that doesn’t strip the skin

The Inkey List cleanser contains zinc which helps to reduce oilyness. It also contains allatoin to calm the skin and glycerin for hydration.

The CeraVe cleanser contains hectorite clay to absorb excess oil and minimise shine. It also contains ceramides to prevent moisture loss and hyaluronic acid for hydration.

In terms of performance, both cleansers got rid of my comedonal acne with consistent use. I use them in the morning or at night (as a second cleanse when double cleansing).

Ultimately, both cleansers earn 5/5 on delivery and efficacy. I have chosen to go with CeraVe because I get more value for my money and the dispenser is slightly more convenient.

Have you tried any of them?

Green pasta sauce

240g fresh spinach leaves
1 tin of chopped tomatoes 
300g silken tofu

2tbsp olive oil
1 heaped tbsp of sun dried tomato paste 

250g sliced mushrooms
1 broccoli chopped to florets 

1 tbsp chopped garlic
Grated Parmesan 

1. Into a blender add the tinned tomatoes, spinach and silken tofu with 1/2 cup of water. Blitz until  smooth sauce forms
2. Heat a pan, add some olive oil and saute you mushrooms and broccoli on a medium heat until cooked
3. Heat another pan with some olive oil, once hot add the garlic and sauté with the sun dried tomato paste. Add the green sauce to this and turn heat to low and cover and cook for 7-10 mins. 
4. Cook your pasta while this is happening 
5. Add the sautéed veg to the sauce and mix well
6. Add in your cooked pasta and mix well
7. Add heaps of Parmesan cheese and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil to finish
8. Enjoy ☺️

#Ad I have partnered with @amgenbiotech to raise awareness on 𝗣𝗟𝗔𝗤𝗨𝗘 𝗣𝗦𝗢𝗥𝗜𝗔𝗦𝗜𝗦⁣
⁣
⁣Plaque psoriasis is an 𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗶𝗺𝗺𝘂𝗻𝗲 𝗱𝗶𝘀𝗲𝗮𝘀𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗼𝗿𝗶𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗮𝘁𝗲𝘀 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗻. It involves an overactive immune response that triggers inflammation throughout the body, and topicals may have limited penetration beyond the skin’s surface.⁣
⁣
For those who are feeling like their symptoms are not sufficiently managed by their current topical routine, 𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘁𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 𝗺𝗮𝘆 𝗯𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗱. Systemic medications work internally to help reduce the systemic inflammation associated with plaque psoriasis. Learn more about oral treatment options at 𝗣𝘀𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴𝗗𝗶𝗳𝗳𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘁.𝗰𝗼𝗺 - linked in my bio.

If your skin is not getting better despite  treatments then it’s worth looking are your detergent. 

A common concern with detergents is their potential to cause adverse skin reactions, such as contact dermatitis, due to exposure to allergens or irritants.

SUji Dhokla/Savoury Semolina Cake

Ingredients

1.5 cups Semolina
1.5 cups Yoghurt (natural set, the more sour the better)
1 tsp green chilli paste
1 tsp ginger paste
1/2 cup water
1 packet ENO /fruit salt, you can alternatively use bicarbonate of soda (1/2 tsp)
1tsp sugar
Salt to taste
2 tbsp vegetable oil

3 tbsp sunflower oil
1 tsp mustard seeds
1/4 tsp asophetida
4-5 curry leaves
2 tsp sesame seeds
2 chopped green chillies
Handful of chopped coriander

Green chutney

1/2 cup greek yoghurt
1 cup fresh coriander
1/4 cup dessicate/fresh coconut
Juice of 1/2 lemon
1/2 cup water (vary to your consistency)
1 tsp cumin seeds
1 clove garlic
Salt & pepper to taste

8 inch cake tin

1. Mix together the semolina and the yoghurt, add in the green chilli and ginger paste, mix well and add 1/4 cup of water. Set aside for at least half an hour, which allows the semolina to swell
2. Grease an 8 inch cake tin well, and set up your steamer, as you can see from the video, I use a cake tin ring and set it up in a large saucepan, and add at least two inches water to bring this to the boil and create steam. 
3. After 30 mins, the batter is ready for the ENO/bicarbonate, but first add another 1/4 cup of cold water and 1 tbsp oil and mix well for at least 2-3 minutes, then add your ENO, you will see it fizz a bit and then you must mix well and fast for 30 seconds 
4. Immediately pour this into the cake tin and spread evenly. Sprinkle a dusting of chilli powder if you wish. Now pop this tin onto of the ring in your steamer. Close lid and steam on high heat for 15 minutes. When done, check with tooth pick that is done, should come out clean.
5. In a small saucepan, heat the oil, once hot, add the mustard seeds, sesame seeds and chillies, and asophetida and curry leaves and let it sizzle on a medium heat for 1 minute
6. Turn off heat and pour this mixture over the cooked Dhokla cake. Garnish with fresh green coriander
7. To make the chutney, pop all the ingredients of the chutney in a food processor and blend until smooth. A nutribullet works well

Enjoy this epic combo and let me know how you get on !

JEERA CAULIFLOWER WINGS 
Cumin and Cauliflower are great friends! You can enjoy them on their ow with a salad, or inside a wrap which how I love them!

Ingredients

2 Cauliflower Heads
2 pinches ground turmeric powder
Salt & pepper to taste
1-2 tbsp olive oil

For the sauce:
6 small/3 large ripe tomatoes
2-3 chopped shallots
2 tbsp garlic paste/puree/chopped garlic
3 tbsp oil
4 tbsp cumin seeds
1/4 tsp turmeric powder
2tsp ground cumin
2 tsp ground coriander
Salt to taste
2 tbsp Kashmiri chilli powder
Chopped coriander for garnish
Squeeze of lemon juice

1. Preheat oven to 180 deg C.
2. Chop the cauliflower from base up into florets/wings, wash and place in oven proof dish, coat in olive oil, add the turmeric and salt and pepper, and mix well. Cook in oven for 15 minutes, or until just softened slightly and golden
3. Heat a pan on medium heat, once hot, add the cumin seeds, toast until they darken in colour a little and release a smokey aroma
4. Transfer to pestle and grind lightly until the seeds break down a little. Set aside
5. Into the same hot pan, add the oil, and once hot, add the chopped shallots and cook until lightly brown, then add the garlic, and cook for a minute, then add the toasted cumin seeds, the turmeric powder, ground cumin and ground coriander powder and cook for a few seconds. Add the chopped tomatoes and salt.  Cook this until the tomatoes soften. Slowly add hot water from a boiled kettle (I added about 1 cup if water in thirds). Cook this sauce fo about 10 minutes on a low to medium heat. 
6. Add the Kashmiri chill powder and mix well
7. Add the cooked cauliflower and turn off heat, coat all the cauliflower in the sauce and garnish with chopped coriander and squeeze of lemon
Enjoy with a salad, in a wrap, or on their won with some yoghurt!

Soya chunk Thai red curry

- 1 cup soya chunks
- 2 cups mixed frozen vegetables (e.g., peas, carrots, corn, green beans etc)
- 1 can (400 ml) coconut milk
- 2 cups sliced white mushrooms
- 2 tablespoons red curry paste
- 1 tablespoon soya sauce (or I used yondu seasoning)
- 1 tablespoon oil
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 teaspoon ginger, minced
- 1 tablespoon lime juice
- Fresh cilantro for garnish
- Salt and pepper to taste

1. Soak the soya chunks in hot water for about 10-15 minutes until they are soft. Drain and squeeze out excess water.

2. Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat.
 - Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent. Add the mushrooms and sauté until light golden
 - Add the minced garlic and ginger, and cook for another minute until fragrant.
 - Stir in the red curry paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, allowing the flavors to blend.

3. Add the drained soya chunks to the pan and stir to coat with the curry paste.
 - Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
 - Add the mixed frozen vegetables, soy sauce, and lime juice. You can also add the veg first before adding the coconut milk
 - Stir well, cover, and let the curry simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the flavours are well combined.

4. Taste the curry and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
 - Garnish with fresh cilantro.
 - Serve hot with steamed rice or noodles.

Enjoy!

Chia seed yoghurt breakfast (inspired by Indian curd rice and raita)

Soak chia seeds 
1/4 cup chia seeds and 1 cup water - mix well and soak overnight

Grated veg
1 grated carrot, half a grated cucumber and 5 grated radishes

2 cups greek yoghurt

For the tempering
1 tbsp cooking oil
1/2 tsp cumin seeds
1/2 tsp mustard seeds
3-4 finely chopped garlic cloves
4-5 curry leaves
A few cashew nuts

Salt
1 tsp Crushed /split mustard seeds 

Fresh coriander leaves
Pomegranate seeds

Mix the yoghurt, soaked chia seeds and grated veg in a large bowl, add salt and the split mustard seeds. Set aside

Heat your pan, add oil, once hot, add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves and garlic - can add a chilli too, and sand back as it splutters, let this sizzle for one minute and then take off the heat
Let the cool a little and then pour this mixture over the chia/yoghurt mix

Mix well, add some chopped fresh coriander and pomegranate seeds to the mix. Enjoy!

This makes for a great breakfast for me, and a great side dish to my evening dinner at times. Keeps me fuller for longer and tastes delicious!

Mixing certain skincare ingredients can lead to irritation, reduced effectiveness, or other adverse reactions. Some formulas will contain these ingredients together but they are made that way and shown to be effective, then that’s fine…

Here are some common ingredient combinations I avoid:

Retinol and Benzoyl Peroxide- Both are powerful acne treatments, but using them together can cause excessive dryness, irritation, and redness. It’s best to use one in the morning and the other at night, or on alternate days.

Vitamin C and AHAs/BHAs- Vitamin C is an effective antioxidant, but mixing it with alpha hydroxy acids (AHAs) or beta hydroxy acids (BHAs) can alter its pH balance, reducing its effectiveness and potentially causing irritation. Use them at different times of the day or on different days.

3. Niacinamide and Vitamin C- While newer formulations may be more stable, traditionally, these two ingredients can neutralise each other’s benefits. If you want to use both, apply them at different times of the day or choose products specifically formulated to contain both.

4. Retinol and AHAs/BHAs- Both retinol and chemical exfoliants like AHAs/BHAs can be very harsh on the skin. Using them together can lead to over-exfoliation, irritation, and increased sensitivity. Alternate their use or choose gentler formulations if combining them in a routine.

5. Benzoyl Peroxide and Vitamin C- Benzoyl peroxide can oxidize vitamin C, rendering it less effective. To avoid this, use benzoyl peroxide in your nighttime routine and vitamin C in the morning.

It’s always best to introduce new products gradually and pay attention to how your skin reacts. If in doubt, consult a derm or a skincare specialist to tailor a skincare routine to your specific needs.

Is there a difference between these two? 

#soap #skincare #skintips

𝗕𝗢𝗕 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗹𝗲𝘆, 𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗰𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗿, 𝗦𝘂𝗻𝘀𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗲𝗻𝘀 & 𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗖𝗵𝗲𝗰𝗸𝘀//⁣
⁣
It’s important for black people to get 𝗿𝗲𝗴𝘂𝗹𝗮𝗿 𝘀𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗰𝗵𝗲𝗰𝗸𝘀 because skin cancers tend to occur on non sun exposed areas of the body. Bob Marley’s case highlights the importance of emphasizing skin checks as much as sun protection in messaging on skin-cancers especially when it comes to communities of color.⁣
⁣
While sun protection measures like wearing sunscreen with SPF can help reduce the risk of other types of skin cancer, they may not directly protect against ALM since it’s not primarily caused by sun exposure. ⁣
#skincaretips #skincare #spf #skincancer

𝗛𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗿 𝗵𝗮𝗱 𝗮 𝘀𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗰𝗵𝗲𝗰𝗸 ?

𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗯𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘃𝘀 𝘀𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗯𝗿𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 : 

👉🏽𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗯𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴: involves using ingredients to lighten the skin tone by 𝗿𝗲𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗲𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗶𝗻 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗻𝘁. Bleaching aims to lighten the skin & can be used to address hyperpigmentation,or achieve a lighter complexion overall.⁣
Tyrosinase inhibitors like hydroquinone, kojic acid etc  are often used for this. ⁣
⁣
👉🏾𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗻 𝗯𝗿𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴: skin brightening focuses on enhancing the skin’s radiance and improving its overall appearance by reducing dullness, evening out skin tone, and diminishing dark spots or blemishes. Skin brightening ingredients include vitamin C, niacinamide, or alpha hydroxy acids (AHAs) that promote skin cell turnover resulting in a more luminous complexion. Unlike bleaching, skin brightening aims for a natural glow without altering the skin’s natural color.⁣

*Both can be used together however in a routine. 
#skincare #skintips #darkspots

Happy Thursday 👋🏽 and thank you for hanging with me in my lil IG corner 🙏🏽 I’ve had over 20k beautiful people hit ‘follow’ in the last week, so I’ll soon be dropping a post to tell you why I show up on Instagram, but for now I wanna get to know you. So I’ll share five things about me with you, in the hope you’ll take the time to share with me too. 
.
1. I’m a strong and resilient woman, who has survived much adversity in my 58 years. However, at this time in my life, I choose to lean ‘all up’ in my softness! 🥰✨ 2. Fun fact: If you look closely enough at the closing scenes in The Little Mermaid 2023, you’ll see me as Disney’s first middle aged, silver haired, black mermaid. Don’t blink! 😉  3. My happy place is being in nature, I go in seek of it wherever I travel. Skinnydipping in a cold saltwater pool in a pine forest in sunny Ibiza surrounded by the scent of orange blossom and pine was definitely a heavenly highlight last year (please scroll for in-feed post). When at home, I regularly cycle to my local park to catch the sunrise.. which I share in my stories.. I’m obsessed! 🌅 4. I’ve been single for a long, looooooong time! Mostly I’m ok with rolling solo, but there are those moments (always when I’m enjoying something alone) that I think “Man! When is he gonna show up, to enjoy this with me?!” 🤔 5. I love music, my dad was a drummer, so I’ve always had music in my home. I never mastered an instrument, but my appreciation for the art form led me to become a music journalist when I was in my 20s, and I can often be found ‘singing’ my heart out and dancing in my kitchen with my grown daughters. 🎶💃🏽My poor neighbours! 🤣 Now your turn? Please share one or two things with me, or ask me a question. Let’s get to know each other? 😘
.
.
.
#miamaugé #notaninfluencer #midlifeblogger #singlelife #musiclover #softlife #grateful #over50 #silverhair #silversister #midlifeempowerment #beautymodel #blackhairstyles #maturemodel

I’m not sure we’re ever “complete”, but my 50s have most definitely been my ‘happy girl era’. After decades of putting others needs above my own, my happiness is finally the priority. Free to do those things I’ve put off for so long. Free to revisit and reignite past passions, and free to say “Yes!” to new experiences. With valuable life lessons learned and immense gratitude for the journey so far, I’m ready, more than ever, for the adventures ahead. 
.
On another note; This audio says “Happy GIRL era”. If you’re a grown a@% woman, how do you feel about being referred to as a girl? I’m interested to know…

‘Normal hair loss’ ’due to age’ and ‘hormones’ can be alarming, and it’s not spoken about enough. I didn’t know about it and I didn’t expect it when it started happening to me some years ago. I notice it mostly when it’s wet.. if my hair didn’t have frizz, which gives me volume, it would be much more noticeable. Thank goodness for frizz! To begin with I was fobbed off by doctors because it didn’t “look thin” to them, I’ve heard that this is a common experience for those of us with textured hair, but I know my hair better than anyone, and I reckon I’ve now lost about 50% around the hairline and on the top. Doctors can also be dismissive because it’s “normal for your age”, I’ve also been told that “there is nothing you can do about it”. If you have any experience with ‘age related’ hair loss, or any tips to share please do so in the comments below. Quick poll: Have you experienced hair loss in midlife?

When I was becoming a woman in the 80s and 90s I felt overlooked and ‘othered’ due to a lack of representation in marketing. I rarely, if ever, saw my skin tone, my hair texture, my curves in magazines or tv commercials. Back then ‘heroin chic’ was walking the runways, and I sure as hell wasn’t that!! When I switched this Instagram account from private to public in 2020, all those boxes had been ticked.. ‘diversity’ was a buzz word, and the beauty of all sizes, skin tones and hair textures was finally visible and beaming from billboards across the land. However, as a midlife woman (I was 54 by then)that familiar feeling of being ignored and othered once again had fully emerged, and it angered me. Why was I being forced, once again, to shop with brands that didn’t see me? That only used young models for their campaigns. This is the main reason for me starting this account in 2020. I identified Instagram as a way to be heard and seen. I wanted to connect with like minded women over 40 who were being visible and vocal in an attempt to make change. To my surprise within 4 weeks of having a public IG page I was scouted by a number of model agencies. “Who me?! I can’t be a professional model! Wait! What?!” was my immediate response if I’m honest. I’ve always been camera shy, but I quickly realised that representation was far more important than any awkwardness I was going to feel front of camera. So I took a deep breath and said “Yes!” Modelling has purpose for me, I know the importance of inclusion and representation from my lived experience, and this is why I do it. So much more work to be done, but I do feel we are making change together! 
.
.
.
.
#representationmatters #ageinclusion matters. 
#maturemodel #miamaugé #silverhair #curlyhair #matureskincare #happyinmyskin #greyhair #over50 #over40 #genx 
.

Here’s a lil behind the scenes peep at a recent shoot I did with them for their ‘True Match’ foundation range. I’m so grateful to be a small part of this powerful campaign, and I’m looking forward to seeing the final edit go live. Stay tuned! 
#ad #AgeInclusivity #representationmatters #truematch #lorealparis #makeup

100k wow! My account hit 100k a few weeks back and I couldn’t quite believe it, so I didn’t mark it with you.. I guess I needed to be sure you were gonna stay with me. I’ve been quietly grafting over here in my little corner of Instagram for the past 4 years. Without any professional creative help, aside from my daughters’ occasional involvement, but they’re busy girls with little interest in social media. So having 100k in four years may seem like a small feat to some, but to me it means the world!! So today I thank you for joining me here, for every kind comment, for your encouragement, and for inspiring me to continue to show up to challenge ageism and the negative narrative around midlife and beyond, to disrupt the fashion and beauty industries and push for age inclusion, and to generally shake up this youth focussed world!! I appreciate you, and truly believe that together we are making a difference, and by us putting in the work for positive change the next generation won’t have to, coz we’ll have shaped a new landscape, a kinder space for them to inherit. We got this!! 
.
.
.
#miamaugé #ageinclusivity #representationmatters #silverhair #over50 #genx #celebrate #100kfollowers #thankyou #grateful #happy #thisis58

.
How do you feel about frizz?

Having 100% even skin is dream I beg people to let go off daily. There are songs may odds stacked against achieving that goal as a darker skin type person. Too many.

Hi lovess, ever wondered how you could minimise enlarged pores? Well, look no further. 

First things first. Your pores do not open and close, but their sizes can fluctuate. Next, pore size is largely genetic, but factors such as oil production and skin elasticity may make them larger. 

Now that we’ve gotten those points out of the way, lets move on to the main gist. There are four ways you could tackle large pores: 

1️⃣ Decrease oil production: The more oil the skin produces, the more dilated the opening of the pores will be, leading to enlarged pores. To fix this, use ingredients that reduce oil production; niacinamide, green tea, retinoids, salicylic acid.

2️⃣ Boost collagen production: As we age, we lose elasticity in our skin, which causes pore openings to appear larger than they are. Certain ingredients encourage the skin to produce more collagen and maintain skin elasticity. Look out for retinoids, peptides, and vitamin c.

3️⃣ Treat and prevent clogged pores: Salicylic acid (BHA) penetrates into the skin and removes all the gunk that causes enlarged pores. AHAs (like lactic acid & glycolic acid) exfoliate the top layer of the skin, preventing clogged pores. Retinoids also target clogged pores.

4️⃣ Protect collagen and elastin: UV exposure breaks down collagen and elastin in the skin, leading to enlarged pores and reduced skin elasticity. To prevent this, always apply a broad spectrum sunscreen during the day.

Ultimately, note that if your pores are genetically large, topical products are unlikely to help. Also, I don’t use all products at once. I personally use BHA to tackle large pores.

Products mentioned:

- @paulaschoice 2% BHA Exfoliating Liquid
- @sachiskin Ursolic Acid & Retinal (PR)
- @beautyofjoseon Glow Serum
- @sundayriley Lactic Acid Treatment
- @blackgirlsunscreen Kids SPF 50

Here are three quick facts about retinal:

1️⃣: Retinal works up to 11 times faster than retinol. 

2️⃣: Retinal has anti-bacterial properties, making it perfect for acne-prone skin. 

3️⃣: Whilst retinol requires two conversion steps before your skin can use it, retinal only requires one step. Consequently, retinal offers quicker results compared to retinol.

Azelaic acid is here to stay because we love a multifunctional queen. This star ingredient fights acne, reduces hyperpigmentation, and calms the skin - what’s not to love?

I particularly love this azelaic acid serum from @nichebeautylab because it is everything good. I personally did not experience any stinging, it is fast-absorbing, and I find it immediately soothing. 

I use this serum everyday with no issues. It helps to keep my comedonal acne at bay. I’ve also noticed that it works well on moderate inflamed acne. As a bonus, it also helps to brighten my dark spots (when used alongside other products).

Some key ingredients of this serum are 15% azelaic acid, ceramides, colloidal oatmeal, and glycerin. Now you can see why I adore this product. You get the benefits of azelaic acid without compromising your skin health. 

This should work for all skin types. If you’re dealing with rosacea and/or redness, you may want to try azelaic acid. Ultimately, if you haven’t used azeliac acid before, be sure to start slow and gradually build your skin’s tolerance.

As an Aesthetician, I take my role of being a bridge between between beauty brands and final consumers seriously.

Here’s the thing. I wish I could pinpoint a massive difference between these two cleansers. Personally, I find them to be pretty similar. 

Both cleansers are super gentle and hydrating. They lather up nicely and leave my skin feeling fresh. They are non-drying and do not irritate my skin. I also use both for a second cleanse when double cleansing.

For the ingredients. Both contain ceramides, hyaluronic acid and cholesterol, all of which support the skin barrier. The oil cleanser contains squalane which helps to soften the skin and protect the skin barrier. Finally unlike the foaming cleanser, the oil cleanser does not contain niacinamide. 

The only major difference I noticed is that the foaming oil cleanser has a slightly thinner consistency, so it tends to run out faster than the original foaming cleanser.

Ultimately I recommend both for people with oily skin and combination skin. People with sensitive skin may also enjoy them.

Have you used any of these cleansers? Share your thoughts with me!

Harrods called and I answered 🤩🥹

I went in for a medical pedicure and it turned out to be the best pedicure I have ever had (thanks to the gorgeous podiatrist, Amber) ❤️.

Unlike the regular pedicure, a medical pedicure is very thorough and specific, paying close attention to the health of your nails and skin, and not just how they look.

Here’s why you should consider a medical pedicure: 

1. It is performed by a licensed medical professional (often a podiatrist).

2. The podiatrist will diagnose any skin or nail concerns that may be present and advise you on how best to manage them.

3. There is a stronger focus and strict regulation on hygiene. All treatments use medical-grade products and freshly sterilised equipments for each client, meaning the risk of picking up an infection is extremely low.

4. The treatment is performed in a private, professional room.

The procedure is ideal for those who have underlying conditions (e.g ingrown toenails, nail fungus, diabetes), or if you simply want to maintain the general health of your feet. I’ll certainly be going back soon!

Do you think you’ll be getting a medical pedi?

Thank you for having me @harrods ❤️

There is always room for new learning or new information, that you are aware doesn’t mean others are. So when next you see someone with darkened knuckles, it may just be a side effect of a medication or a metabolic disorder. 

And if you out of no where start seeing your knuckles getting darker, first instinct shouldn’t be “How do I get rid of it” it should be “What is the cause” 

Not only will finding the cause make treatment easier, it may even be expose you to whole new world of medical discoveries that may save your life.

There are some local differences but if you’re in Italy, France, Spain etc some of the best in class sunscreens are #larocheposay, #avene, #vichy, and #isdin. 

There are other amazing brands (SVR for example), but if you’re looking to stock up you’re going to have great luck at just about any pharmacy!

Why do I care so much about sunscreen? To me it’s the foundation of a house. Without it, what are we even doing here.

It’s wild that decades after I first heard the word base tan we are arguing over facts vs progress but here we are. Base tans aren’t a thing, you aren’t building ‘tolerance’ which is good for you… your body is doing its best against your choices 😭

Nuance with everything— can some people tolerate a tan more than others? Absolutely. Does it mean it’s universally good for you? Nope. Dose makes the poison and the thing with the sun is that varies by person and genetics. What drives me up the wall is people with my skin tone promoting tanning. Please stop.

Also, as someone who used to believe in these things, my frustration is aimed at the people spreading this misinformation, not the people who believe it!

#skincare #tanning #safetanning #sunscreen #nontoxic

Whenever I read an article I tend to break it down like this in my head, screenshot and share any other headlines you want broken down because this isn’t a NYT problem (don’t even blame them or any media company at this point) but a platform and human problem.

✨Rules, rules, rules…
How constrained do you feel?

For me, I’ve decided that I won’t be ignored, become invisible or give-in to the age-old view that women over 50 are less productive or less worthy of attention.

Until recently, around the time of my silver hair transition, I felt like I was expected to ‘be’ a certain way as I aged. Now, I see it with different eyes - wide open ones. 

I wear what I am comfortable in, apply makeup in whatever colour or style I like, ignore anti-ageing messages and try to inject as much fun as I possibly can into my journey. 

Having been inspired by a community of women online who refuse to be sidelined and ignored #silversisters , I have embraced my inner passion for life and now act and react according to how I feel, and not how I think society would expect.

✨Are you the same? How are you disrupting the ‘norm’?Comment below - I’d love to know. And let’s stay visible! 

Julie

Many of you have been waiting patiently for this review, but I really wanted to properly test it out many times, through many washes because it’s been too good to be true so far.

In my latest YT video I share how age and hormones have been impacting my scalp and hair. For yonks I’ve been whining on my channel about dealing with a sensitive scalp and how scalp care = hair care. This started in my 40’s and I only recently discovered it’s a symptom of perimenopause. Turns out that hormonal fluctuations can cause skin on the scalp to thin and weaken the protective barrier. It’s gotten to a point where coloring my roots causes so much discomfort and side effects that I try to avoid it. 

This is my quick review of @seedbee_official Water Colour Gray Hair Cover for root touch-ups which (not to be dramatic) has changed my life. It’s not available outside of Korea (avail @oliveyoung_official ) but I’ve been re-purchasing from Amazon - go to link in bio under Shop My Post. This works as well as anything I’ve tried, covers even better than traditional semi-permanent dyes and has been tested to last through 300 washes. My Asian hair has extremely resistant grays and I’ve not experienced any fading, However, this doesn’t stain the skin either so no shadow around the hairline.

There are also no strong chemical fumes that sting my eyes. No burning of the scalp during application, no dandruff afterwards and this doesn’t change the texture of my hair which remains soft. This powder with its 36 natural scalp and hair conditioning ingredients, biotin from France and colouring from 8 types of black foods, is activated only using water. 

I don’t know what kind of sorcery this is but i’m here for it!

I use Natural Black but also liked Dark Brown for softer coverage. 

Purchased and repurchased gajillion times

I absolutely love conversations that centres darker skin types!

This isn’t a new concern in the skincare world, it’s been flogged and beaten, But what is evident is that a lot hasn’t changed as regards centering darker skin types in research. Like my friend @psalmuel.josephs always say, “no one will do it for us”

The only paper o found that actually corroborates my stand is one funded by the L’Oréal group, that brings into focus founders bias. However, that doesn’t negate everything in the study. 

What we haven’t found however is a conclusive study that says vitamin D deficiency is caused by SPF usage. All concerns raised are mere conjectures. 

If the sole reason for advocating against sunscreen usage in darker skin types is vitamin D deficiency, I think we already have a solution to that. Vitamin D supplements. 

Until we have data that suggests otherwise, wear your sunscreen 

#sunscreen.

Try this if you’re dealing with mild to moderate (inflamed) acne. Includes benzoyl peroxide, ceramides, niacinamide, hyaluronic acid.

⭐️ Foaming Cleanser: This was the first cleanser I bought when I started my skincare journey and it’s remained a staple since then. This one has a thick gel texture that lathers easily. It could work for all skin types, especially oily skin. Perfect for the nights I don’t feel like double-cleansing. Includes ceramides, niacinamide, hyaluronic acid, cholesterol. 

⭐️ SA Renewing Cleanser: This is your go-to if you’re dealing with whiteheads, blackheads or skin congestion. It is super gentle and doesn’t strip away the skin’s natural oils. Like the foaming cleanser, this has a gel texture that lathers easily. Perfect for oily/acne prone skin. Includes salicylic acid, ceramides, niacinamide, cholesterol. 

⭐️ Hydrating Foaming Oil Cleanser: I bought this to replace the foaming cleanser and I’m glad I wasn’t disappointed. It’s similar to the foaming cleanser but has a thin gel texture. This leaves my skin feeling smooth and hydrated. You can use it regardless of your skin type. I use this as a second cleanse when double cleansing. Includes ceramides, squalane, cholesterol, hyaluronic acid.
