Each type of acne typically requires a different ingredient to fight it. Here are all the products I use to target various types of acne: 

~ @Cerave Acne Foaming Cream Cleanser. Benzoyl peroxide is a star ingredient for treating inflamed acne. This cleanser gets rid of my pimples within a few days. I use it 3-4 times a week when my skin needs it.

~ @paulaschoice 2% BHA Liquid Exfoliant. My go-to salicylic acid product for unclogging pores and treating comedones (whiteheads/blackheads). I use this 1-2 times a week.

~ @deciem Lactic Acid. This helps to clear rough and bumpy texture. While salicylic acid works within the pores, AHAs like lactic acid work on the surface of the skin to help improve skin texture and reveal radiant skin. I use it 1-2 times a week.

~ @theinkeylist Retinol Serum. Ideally, when targeting acne, AHAs and retinol offer similar benefits, so you can stick with one of them. My skin has been behaving for a while now so I prefer to use retinol to target my overall skin appearance. You don’t see immediate results, but retinol/retinoids offer better long-term benefits. I use this 2-4 times a week (largely because the percentage of retinol is relatively low).

I mostly use Paula’s Choice’s BHA because regularly unclogging my pores helps to prevent inflamed acne, comedones and uneven texture. In any case, I don’t exfoliate more than twice a week. You should only use actives like these when your skin barrier is healthy.

While people with darker skin tones have more natural protection against the sun due to higher levels of melanin, they can still get sunburned if they are exposed to intense sunlight for prolonged periods without protection. ⁣
⁣
The sunburns in darker skin folks can be severe because the false sense of protection means they often ignore early warning signs that skin is actively burning. ⁣
⁣
It’s important for everyone, regardless of skin color, to take precautions against sunburn by :⁣
✔️using sunscreen, ⁣
✔️wearing protective clothing, and ⁣
✔️seeking shade when necessary

My skin is most healthy when it looks dewy. Not oily or shiny, just dewy. Dewy skin is supple, hydrated and moisturised, creating a glow from inside out.

Here’s how I achieve dewy skin:

1️⃣ Hydration: There should be some water beneath the skin surface to create a moist appearance. The dewy look is a result of light bouncing off the water retained by the skin cells. The key is to apply your hydrating products on damp skin. My @cosrx Snail Mucin Essence is a 10/10 hydrator.

2️⃣ Exfoliation: You do not need to exfoliate if you don’t want to. But a build-up of dead skin cells can make even the most dewy skin look dull. Chemical exfoliants encourage the skin to shed dead skin cells and replace them with newer ones, revealing a dewy and radiant skin. Lactic acid is my go-to exfoliant. I use it 1-2 times a week.

3️⃣ Sunscreen: The biggest obstacle to a healthy skin is sun damage. In fact, prolonged UV exposure dehydrates the skin, making it appear dull. Make sure you’re wearing sunscreen and avoiding sun exposure, where possible. My current spf is the @sosserum SPF 50 Suncream. 

4️⃣ Healthy skin barrier: Your skin barrier keeps the bad guys out and locks in the good guys. When it is compromised, your skin will look dull. Avoid over-exfoliating, buy gentle cleansers, and use multifaceted moisturisers (containing humectants and emollients).

𝗜𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘂𝗽𝗸𝗲𝗲𝗽 𝗼𝗳 𝗻𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗵𝗮𝗶𝗿 𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗹𝘆 ? ⁣
⁣
A girlfriend hit me up to let me know she was returning to relaxers aka the “𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗺𝘆 𝗰𝗿𝗮𝗰𝗸” as she put it. She was letting me know because she knew I “𝘄𝗮𝘀 𝗴𝗼𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗼 𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝘀𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗼 𝘀𝗮𝘆.” So she figured, she would prepare & warn me she didn’t need no lectures from her dermatologist friend. 

There is a “𝘁𝗲𝘅𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝘁𝗮𝘅” on natural hair products, she lamented. “They are more expensive and require a lot to really care for my hair.” After 12 years of running through expensive natural hair products, natural hair stylists, countless youtube vids and classes to learn how to do her hair, it was time to hang it up. “It’s a recession and something has to give. 𝗡𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗵𝗮𝗶𝗿 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗶𝗺𝗽𝗹𝘆 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗶𝘁’𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗴𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗯𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿.” ⁣
⁣
Naturally, I did have something  to say lol… but I want to know how many of you can identify with her. Is natural hair really that expensive to care for ? 

#skincaretips #skincare #naturalhair

⁣
𝗛𝗲𝗮𝘁 𝗗𝗮𝗺𝗮𝗴𝗲//𝗪𝗮𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗲𝘅𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝘆𝗼𝘂? ⁣
⁣
In the era of silk presses, you have to make sure those styling your hair are not using excessive heat to accomplish bone straight hair. I really love how @transformedbyo blow dries my hair. It’s one of the reasons I go to her. She always manages to do it with very little heat as possible. ⁣
⁣
𝗛𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗳𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗼𝗻 𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗵𝗲𝗮𝘁 𝗱𝗮𝗺𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝗵𝗮𝗶𝗿:⁣
⁣
⛔️. **Split Ends:** Heat damage can cause the ends of hair strands to split, resulting in a frayed appearance.⁣
⛔️. **Dryness:** hair is stripped of its natural oils, leaving it dry, brittle, and lacking moisture.⁣
⛔️ **Dullness:** Hair appears lackluster, losing its natural shine and vibrancy.⁣
⛔️. **Brittleness:** Hair becomes weak and prone to breakage.⁣
⛔️ **Changes in Texture:** Heat damage can alter the hair’s natural texture, resulting in a rough, straw-like feel, particularly in areas frequently styled with heat tools.⁣

#hair #hairloss #silkpress #naturalhair

Creamy Yoghurt Chicken curry

Ingredients

1-2 tbsp cooking oil
1 large onion finely chopped
2 tbsp chopped garlic
1 tsp cumin powder
1 tsp coriander powder
1/2 tsp turmeric powder
1tbsp Kashmiri chilli powder
1 tbsp tomato puree/paste/concetnrate
3-4 large ripe tomatoes finely chopped (or used tinned)
1 tbsp garam masala
1 tbsp kasuri methi

4 chicken breasts, cubed to bite size pieces

Marinade : 1.5 cups greek yoghurt, 1/2 tsp turmeric, salt, black pepper, 1 tbsp garam masala, squeeze of little lemon juice

1. Mix together the marinade ingredients and marinade the chicken in this yoghurt mix while you make the base
2. Heat pan, add oil and once hot add the chopped onions, let them tuen light brown in colour, then add the garlic, chopped tomatoes, tomato puree and turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and Kashmir chilli. add a pinch of salt. Maintain medium heat and continue stirring and cooking to 5-10 mins unwell the oil separates (See video)
3. Turn heat on low
4. Add the marinaded chicken to the pan, and mix gently, let this warm up slowly and then bring to a gradual simmer and then cover and cook for 10-15 mins or until the chicken is cooked through
5. Add the garam masala, kasuri methi and salt, mix well and taste. Cook uncovered for 5 minutes and add a finished touch of a little butter before serving. ENJOY!

#cookingtime #midweek #recipeoftheday #curry #healthyfood #healthylifestyle #cookingforkids

Spinach, chickpea and paneer curry

Ingredients:
- 1-2 tbsp oil
- 1/2 tsp mustard seeds
- 1/2 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 large onion, chopped
- 1 tbsp garlic, chopped
- 250g spinach
- 1 can tinned tomatoes
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 can chickpeas, drained
- 250g paneer, cubed
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1/2 tsp ground cumin
- 1/2 tsp ground coriander
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 tbsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves)
- 1 tbsp butter
- Salt to taste
- 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder

Instructions:
1. Prepare the Base: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add mustard seeds and cumin seeds, and let them sizzle. Then, sauté onions and garlic until they start to brown.
2. Process Spinach and Tomatoes: In a food processor, blend spinach and tinned tomatoes until smooth. Set aside.
3. Cook Tomato Paste: Add tomato paste to the pan, stir, and cook for a few seconds.
4. Brown the Paneer: Add paneer cubes to the pan, letting them brown slightly. Then, add chickpeas, turmeric, ground cumin, and ground coriander. Stir well to coat everything with the spices.
5. Add Spinach Puree: Pour in the spinach-tomato mixture and stir.
6. Simmer: Cover and let the curry simmer on medium heat for 10-15 minutes.
7. Season: Add salt, Kashmiri chili powder, kasuri methi, and garam masala. Mix well to incorporate all the flavors.
8. Finish and Serve: Serve hot, ideally with naan or rice.

AD Picking the sunscreen from the shelves can be overwhelming, but don’t let it stop you… here are some of my top tips…

1.⁠ ⁠Choose Broad-Spectrum: Look for labels that say ‘broad-spectrum’ to protect against both UVA, UVB rays and even Blue Light!
2.⁠ ⁠SPF Matters: Use a sunscreen with at least SPF 30. Higher SPF offers more protection but remember, no sunscreen blocks 100% of UV rays.
3.⁠ ⁠Right Amount: Apply about a teaspoon on your face or two finger lengths worth. 
4.⁠ ⁠Formulation: some sunscreens are better for certain skin concerns - added beneficial ingredients like vitamin C or niacinamide can help with existing skin issues as well as protecting.
5.⁠ ⁠Reapply Regularly: Every two hours if you’re directly exposed to sunlight, or after sweating or swimming.
6.⁠ ⁠Daily Habit: Make it a part of your daily routine, even on cloudy days or when indoors near windows.

Mung Bean Sprout Bhel (salad)

1 cup dried whole mung beans
2 tbsp cooking oil
1/2 tsp mustard seeds
1/2 tsp cumin seeds
1/2 tsp turmeric powder
Salt
3-4 cloves of garlic (finely sliced)
1 lemon
1/4 tsp asoephetida 
1/2 tsp mango powder
1 chopped tomato
1 Finley chopped red onion
Handful of chopped fresh coriander
Handful of pomegranate seeds
1/2 tsp chaat masala
1 dry roasted papad

NOTE: In order to enjoy this you will need pre-empt your desire to eat it. The Mung sprouts will need a day or two 

1. To sprout the mung beans, first rinse them in warm water well, and soak them for 8 hours or so
2. Drain all the water and place beans in a self draining container, in a warm dark place. The ideal environment for germination is: warmth, darkness and minimal moisture (the water needs to drain away)
3. To cook the mung beans: heat a pan, add the oil, then add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and chopped garlic, let this sizzle  and then add the asophetida, mix well and while on medium heat, add the turmeric powder
4. Give it a good mix and add the the sprouted mung and 1/2 cup of water. Mix well, add salt and cover and cook for 5-10 minutes
5. Uncover and add a squeeze of lemon, and the mongo powder, check salt and let it cool a little
6. To assemble the bhel/salad, mix the masala sprouts with the tomato, red onion, coriander, pomegranate seeds, and crush the papad in too. Sprinkle with chaat masala and enjoy!

Yes, there is typically a delay between sun exposure and the appearance of sun spots (also known as solar lentigines or liver spots). These spots are the result of the skin’s increased production of melanin, which is the pigment that gives skin its color, as a response to prolonged exposure to ultraviolet (UV) radiation. 

The delay between exposure and the appearance of sun spots can vary widely depending on several factors, including an individual’s skin type, the intensity and duration of sun exposure, and their age. Sun spots may appear years after the initial sun damage has occurred, especially as the skin naturally ages and is less able to repair itself.

In many cases, sun spots can begin to develop or become more pronounced as a person reaches their 40s or 50s, but they can also appear earlier in those who have had extensive sun exposure at a younger age. Regular use of sunscreen and protective clothing can help minimize the formation of new sun spots and other signs of sun damage.

I decided to carve out my deserved space in society, and now at 58 I can honestly say my 50s have seen some of the most incredible years of my life! The power is all in the mindset!

The stigma needs to stop… the singleton stigma that exists whatever age you are. People often speculate, when there are many valid reasons why a person might be single. Then there is the pressure to be in a relationship! I felt it mostly in my mid to late 20s when “the biological clock was ticking”, and now I choose to ignore the pressure in my 50s, when the pressure is that I need someone to ‘grow old with’. The truth is I would absolutely love to be in a relationship, and perhaps I’m not proactive enough, but I’m hopeful I’ll find my match…. That he’ll come along, and we will enrich one other. Until then I’m fulfilled, having fun and living my life just as it is. 
.
Thank you @traceeellisross for these words.
.

When I began this Instagram account in 2020, it was because I was fed up of being ignored by the fashion and beauty industries. As a consumer I felt I deserved to see representation that looked like me in marketing. Back in the 1980s and 90s, never seeing my body type, rarely seeing my hair texture and my skin tone in mainstream marketing made me feel ‘othered’ as a young woman. Then at 54 in 2020 it seemed all the above ‘diversity’ boxes had been ticked, but now I wasn’t seeing my age group, I had spent my 40s, not seeing any representation that looked like me. Enough was enough! I didn’t want to be made to feel like that a second time around, so I decided to be visible and vocal on Instagram. I simply wanted to see if by connecting with others who felt like I did we could make a change, so I went from private to public with my IG account to try to shake things up a little. I just never imagined it would lead to me actually being in the campaigns! 4 years on and it still blows my mind! Life truly can take you down a new path at any age! I really hope that when you see me you somehow see yourself too, that is why I do what I do! 
.
.
Shout out to @augustinusbader who have been age inclusive with their wonderful inter generational casting since their UK launch, this recent hair care campaign is testament to that, and to the @harrodsbeauty staff who kindly put up with me and my daughter shooting this reel. 
.
#silverhair #silversisters #miamaugé #curlyhair #over50model #over40 #midlifejourney #greyhairmovement

Life is too precious to be put on hold because of societal beauty standards. I’ve gained weight since the fractured ankle, and now with my painful frozen shoulder I’ve not been able to exercise and regain any notable level of fitness yet. When the weight started creeping on I responded by feeling low… my lifelong programmed response. I turned down work and play, and generally wanting to hide at home until I could get active again. More recently I’ve decided that life is too precious to be put on hold, I’ve decided to embrace my new curves, I don’t know how long I’ll have them for so why not just have fun with them, and continue to enjoy life to the fullest? And what better way to debut them than wearing a shimmering silver co-ord from @neverfullydressed (gifted) to the @poawawards at @devere_grand_connaught_rooms on Mother’s Day evening? I can’t lie, I felt a lil self conscious to begin with, but soon pushed those feelings aside. A changing body at this time in life is always going to take some adjustment. Anyway… who knows? I might just keep the curves! 
.
.
.

I remember when I first got into makeup via so-called” beauty gurus” on YouTube. This was back in 2016 in all its 2016 makeup glory. Drying matte liquid lipstick, sharp brows, smokey warm eyeshadow looks, a beaming highlight, and (of course) a seemingly flawless near-to-perfection base. Not a pore in sight, not to even mention any visible texture.

This was a few years before I got my severe acne in 2018, but I still had some acne scars lingering infatuated after minor acne breakouts. I remember watching those tutorials with awe and noting the products in my notes. Surely if a foundation could look that good on a high-definition camera on a YouTuber, surely it would have the same effect on my skin? This was before I knew that face-smoothing effects and filters were used in a lot of those videos. I was confused as to why my skin didn’t look as “flawless” when I used the same products.

Fast forward a few years and face smoothing filters, and editing apps became more and more commonly used on Instagram. With the tap of a finger, anyone could turn any texture into smooth poreless “perfection”. By this time I still struggled with comparison when it came to my skin, but to a whole other level. I had developed severe acne and scrolling the feed and tapping through stories with filters was disheartening.

All of this to say, I know I tend to nag on and on about unfiltered skin in feeds on social media. But it’s because I know from experience that it means a lot to see someone that you can identify yourself with, no matter how trivial it may seem to someone who hasn’t lived through that experience.

Here’s to real unfiltered skin, I see it so often now. And it’s beautiful to see it in all its glory🪩
🧚The art of reframing what you once thought made you less than🧚

Harsh winds/rain/snow can’t bring me down with this routine that I’ve been loving lately. Take it as your sign to simplify your routine and cherish the basics🫶🏼Have an amazing weekend you all🛁

If your skin is feeling tight, irritated, dehydrated and itchy, your skin barrier may be damaged. That’s your sign to go back to the basics. You should:

1. Avoid active ingredients like retinoids, exfoliants, and vitamin C
2. Focus on hydrating, moisturising and protecting the skin

These are my go-to products for restoring my skin barrier:

⭐️ @aveenouk Nourishing Oat Cleanser. This works like a charm and doesn’t strip my skin

⭐️ @cosrx Snail Mucin Essence. My all time favourite hydrating product

⭐️ @illiyoon_official Ceramide Ato Cream. Ceramides help to maintain cell adhesion, thereby preventing moisture loss. I love using this moisturiser at night

⭐️ @aveenouk Oat Gel Moisturiser. Colloidal oatmeal hydrates the skin and soothes irritation. This is my favourite lightweight moisturiser to wear underneath my sunscreen

⭐️ @sosserum SPF 50 Suncream. The simple and gentle formulation is a massive win for sensitised skin

⭐️ @vaseline Petroleum Jelly. Perfect for sealing in moisture and preventing transepidermal water loss

Following on from my recent post, here’s how I incorporate my hyperpigmentation products into my routine. 

I usually use my alpha arbutin and tranexamic acid serums twice a day. Both ingredients are gentle enough to be used everyday, as long as your skin can tolerate it. 

To accelerate the fading process, I use my lactic acid 1-2 times a week. Then I use my retinol serum on the days that I don’t use the lactic acid. That’s pretty much it.

The products in order of application:

~ @aveenouk Nourishing Oat Cleanser
~ @cosrx Snail Mucin Essence
~ @deciem Alpha Arbutin
~ @superdrug Tranexamic Acid Booster
~ @sosserum SPF 50 Sun Cream

Gentle reminder that it can take anywhere from 2 months to a year to completely treat hyperpigmentation. Also remember that sunscreen is non-negotiable when trying to fade dark spots.

👩🏿‍⚕️ How to avoid the ‘retinol scaries’ with these top tips:

Retinoids are skin superstars, boosting collagen production, improving skin texture, fading dark spots, fixing acne, and reducing fine lines. By following these strategies, you can reap the benefits of retinoids and avoid the drying, peeling, irritation, and hypersensitivity known as the retinol scaries.

👩🏿‍⚕️ Use the retinol sandwich method: apply moisturizer before and after retinol to minimize irritation.
👩🏿‍⚕️ Go low and slow: gradually increase retinol concentration to build tolerance.
👩🏿‍⚕️ Avoid combining with other irritating actives to prevent overloading your skin.

#SkinCareTips #RetinolTips #HealthySkin #retinolsandwichmethod 💆🏿‍♀️✨

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝗩𝗮𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗱𝗼𝘂𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 ?The process of intravaginal cleansing with a liquid solution. ⁣
⁣
👉🏾𝗥𝗲𝗮𝘀𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝘄𝗵𝘆 𝗽𝗲𝗼𝗽𝗹𝗲 𝗱𝗼𝘂𝗰𝗵𝗲: To feel cleaner or fresher, or to remove odors. However, the vagina is self-cleaning and generally does not require douching for hygiene purposes. 
⁣
⛔️𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵 𝗿𝗶𝘀𝗸 𝗛𝗣𝗩 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀: There are studies associating douching with high risk HPV infections. “It’s hypothesized that “douching may cause physical epithelium abrasions or disrupt the normal protective vaginal microenvironment, thus facilitating HPV acquisition.” 

PMID: 27553042⁣
PMID: 38357485
#skintips #skincare 
Credit: @grandmaida82 
⁣
𝗗𝗶𝗱 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗸𝗻𝗼𝘄 𝘁𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗗𝗼𝘂𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝘀 𝗵𝗮𝗿𝗺𝗳𝘂𝗹? ⁣

𝗧𝗶𝗽𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗕𝗼𝗱𝘆 𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗲//⁣Body Blemishes and Dull Skin
⁣
𝗖𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗻𝘀𝗲: Go for more gentle non moisture stripping body washes if using exfoliative toners or creams ⁣
⁣
𝗘𝘅𝗳𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗧𝗼𝗻𝗲𝗿 𝗼𝗿 𝗕𝗼𝗱𝘆 𝗖𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗺: Exfoliative  toners containing AHAs or BHAs brighten and promote cell turnover. I like toners or sprays because they are lightweight but exfoliative body creams are great as well. ⁣
⁣
𝗔𝗽𝗽𝗹𝘆 𝗕𝗼𝗱𝘆 𝗥𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗼𝗶𝗱: to fade discoloration and stimulate collagen production. (See link in bio for favs) ⁣
⁣
𝗠𝗼𝗶𝘀𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗶𝘇𝗲: Hydrate the skin with ingredients like hyaluronic acid and ceramides especially when using exfoliants.⁣
⁣
𝗦𝘂𝗻𝘀𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗲𝗻: Finish with a broad-spectrum sunscreen with SPF 30 or higher to protect against UV damage and prevent further discoloration. 

#bodycare #hyperpigmentation #exfoliate #skincaretips

𝗥𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗼𝗶𝗱 𝗦𝗵𝗼𝗿𝘁 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗰𝘁 𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗼𝗱//⁣
⁣
If your skin is sensitive to retinoids, consider using the :⁣
⁣
𝗦𝗵𝗼𝗿𝘁-𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗰𝘁 𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗼𝗱: apply a thin layer of the retinoid product to your face at night, and leave it on like a face mask (for about 20 -30 minutes), then rinse the product off with a gentle cleanser before applying moisturizer or other skincare. ⁣
⁣
𝗡𝗼𝘁𝗲: Efficacy on the short contact method were done with retinoic acid not otc retinoids. However, I have had my patients try it with just about every other retinoid type and seen them tolerate it better that way while still getting improvement in skin texture & even acne. ⁣
⁣
𝗪𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗯𝗲 𝘁𝗿𝘆𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗼𝗱 ?

𝗧𝗶𝗽𝘀 & 𝗗𝗿𝘂𝗴 𝗦𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗦𝗵𝗮𝗺𝗽𝗼𝗼𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗗𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗿𝘂𝗳𝗳 & 𝗦𝗲𝗯𝗼𝗿𝗿𝗵𝗲𝗶𝗰 𝗗𝗲𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝘀 //⁣
⁣
𝗨𝘀𝗲 𝗺𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝘀𝗵𝗮𝗺𝗽𝗼𝗼𝘀: Opt for shampoos containing:⁣
✔️ketoconazole⁣
✔️selenium sulfide ⁣
✔️Coal tar⁣
✔️Sulfur⁣
✔️Zinc pyrithione ⁣
✔️Salicylic acid to combat dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis effectively.⁣
- 𝗜𝗻𝗰𝗼𝗿𝗽𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗶-𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗹𝗮𝗺𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗶𝗻𝗴𝗿𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀: Look for products with soothing ingredients like aloe vera to calm scalp irritation
- 𝗦𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗽 𝗲𝘅𝗳𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Gentle exfoliation helps remove dead skin cells and prevent scalp buildup, reducing the risk of dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis flare-ups.⁣
- 𝗠𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗻 𝗴𝗼𝗼𝗱 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗽 𝗵𝘆𝗴𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗲: by washing regularly and avoiding excessive use of styling products, which can exacerbate dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis.⁣

𝗗: 𝗥𝗼𝘀𝗮𝗰𝗲𝗮:✅⁣
⁣
Rosacea is an inflammatory skin condition marked by redness and tiny blood vessels, often associated  as affecting only lighter skin tones, but it can also impact darker skin. It’s commonly misdiagnosed as acne. Symptoms include stinging, burning, and warmth sensations, along with eye issues. Unlike acne, it doesn’t lead to post-inflammatory changes. Sun exposure makes symptoms, worse. patients may have sensitivity to skincare products and medications.

Friday Pop Quiz: Can you guess the diagnosis ? Pick one best answer & tell me your reason. 

Image cred: Cutis . 2019 Jul;104(1):38-41.

Serums packed with antioxidants ✨ here’s 8 serums that I enjoy using & more importantly that my skin loves .. these are packed with ingredients that are rich in antioxidant properties. Ever since I entered my 30’s I have been focusing a lot on introducing products that are high in antioxidants into my routine. Of course these serums may differ from one another in overall benefits since they have different ingredients but here’s what you can expect from using antioxidants:

• They prevent the effects of pollution (wrinkles, lack lustre complexion)
• They protect skin from environmental damage and ageing
• Can improve skin’s firmness 
• Some antioxidants brighten and improve dull, uneven skin tone (like vitamin C & rice extract)
• Antioxidants like heartleaf, centella, green tea soothe the skin (great for sensitive skin or if you suffer from redness) 
• They help to visibly reduce and improve the appearance of wrinkles

Anywayy, just here to remind you that sunscreen alone is not enough to protect your skin from sun damage. To the extent you can, you should also:

- Seek shade during peak sun hours (10am -4pm)
- Wear UV-blocking sunglasses
- Wear sun-protective clothing
- Wear a wide-brimmed hat

Don’t ask me whether I do all of the above. I’m just telling you the right thing to do 😭🤲🏽.

Pro tip: you can tell whether sunglasses offer UV protection by checking if the frame has a CE or UV400 mark. The sunglasses do not have to be expensive, and the darkness of the lenses have nothing to do with the UV protection.

The real risk is that some sunglasses falsely claim to offer UV protection. May we not fall victim to such.

Do you use other sun protection measures?🥰

As an oily skin babe, it’s important to me that I control sebum (oil) production and reduce the shine on my face. Here are a few ingredients I look out for:

1️⃣ Salicylic acid: salicylic acid is lipophilic, meaning it is able to dissolve oil. This allows it to penetrate into the pores and cleanse away excess oil.

2️⃣ Niacinamide: helps to regulate oil production in the skin. It boosts ceramide production which allows the skin to remain hydrated and moisturised. Therefore, sebum does not need to overcompensate for skin dryness.

3️⃣ Clay (kaolin/bentonite): helps to absorb oil from the surface of the skin and within the pores. This reduces the appearance of shininess.

4️⃣ Retinoids: helps to suppress oil production and shrink the oil gland.

I personally use niacinamide and salicylic acid to reduce oil and shine. What about you?

Circa 1988 - 22 years old - full on Kelly Le Brock bubble perm but my own colour ☺️

I won’t find my first silver for another year so haven’t a clue here about the years of stress I’m going to feel around grey hairs showing.

I’d tell her she’ll spend a fortune to keep something amazing covered up, along with a part of herself that is bursting to get out. 

Fast forward and I’m so thankful I finally got here! 😂 

Freedom is a wonderful thing isn’t it?

AD in collaboration with @larocheposay ☁️ let’s get unready while I talk you through what I wished someone told me while I was struggling with my skin. And giving you blemish-prone ppl some product recommendations, of course🤝

I remember so well how hard it was to navigate skincare at that time. I kept so many products on rotation and it’s safe to say it irritated my skin far more than it helped. I can’t stress enough how the effaclar series has helped me throughout my acne journey.

While I was on medication the effaclar H-iso biome creme was the only moisturizer that could calm my skin. And now the Effaclar duo + M ( with a new and improved formula!!) along with the Effaclar micro peeling cleanser helps whenever my skin is acting up.

I also wish that beyond skincare that you take care of yourselves mentally. Navigating a skin condition is hard work, but please never feel like you need to hide away. You’re so worthy of a full life while navigating your skin journey🩵 #larocheposay #skincareroutine #sensitiveskin #effaclar

✨ Just the lip balm…

My skin is not perfect, and what’s great is that it doesn’t have to be. It has wrinkly bits, enlarged pores, thread veins and rosacea scarring, but It’s protected me for 57 years and counting and I’m super thankful for that. 

For transparency, I LOVE clean makeup and the creativity it allows, but I’m ok with little or no makeup too. 

Tina - the wonderful @grey.grace.andgrit has started this project to showcase regular skin on social media. Send her your video or photo and she’ll share it because it’s important to be able to see normal unfiltered skin. This also kicks back at the ‘anti-ageing’ message the beauty companies bombard us with. It’s okay to have ageing skin. It really is. 

It would be easy to feel bad if your skin isn’t flawless, but don’t! Most skin on social media is filmed in good light or with filters/adjustments that help it to look better. It’s not always real.

So this is me - no makeup, no filters. I didn’t even curl my lashes 😂🤓

Much love J x✨🤍

I’ve always shied away from skin peels, but I was curious to try the new @environskincareuk TCA Cool Peel, known as the ‘peel that doesn’t peel.’ I fully embrace Environ’s approach to skin health, about driving visible results, all whilst protecting the skin barrier. When it comes to this peel, there is a ‘sweet spot’ where the acid will stimulate the keratinocytes in the skin just enough to release growth factors, without stripping or irritating. These growth factors plump and rejuvenate the skin, promoting new collagen and elastin production. I can attest that I had zero redness or irritation - a teeny bit of dryness after 3 days, which subsided overnight to reveal baby smooth, more even, glowier skin.
Environ recommends a course of 6 peel treatments for optimal results. However, results are already noticeable after just one session. My facial was with @joanneevansskinmatters.
Head to the salon finder environskincare.co.uk for your nearest treatment ad

𝟰 𝗧𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘀 𝗧𝗼 𝗟𝗼𝗼𝗸 𝗢𝘂𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗪𝗵𝗲𝗻 𝗦𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗥𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗼𝗶𝗱𝘀//⁣

⭐️𝗥𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗼𝗶𝗱 𝗧𝘆𝗽𝗲:There are several  types with differences in potency, stability, and mechanism of action; some OTC, some prescription.⁣

⭐️𝗣𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗻𝗰𝘆: Determines strength and effectiveness; higher potency may lead to faster results but higher risk of irritation.⁣

⭐️𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗺𝘂𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Look for retinoids with a supportive cast of moisturizing ingredient like hyaluronic acid & ceramides. Base ingredients and texture affect delivery & absorption; choose based on skin type and preferences.⁣

⭐️𝗗𝗲𝗹𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘆 𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗼𝗱: Determines how active ingredient is released and penetrates skin; impacts stability, bioavailability, and tolerability. Look for words like “micro encapsulated” which allows for slow release of the active ingredient, minimizing irritation.

#skintips #skincare #retinol

Gentle reminder to treat your acne first if you’re battling acne and hyperpigmentation. You need to get the acne under control first otherwise you would be dealing with an endless cycle of new pimples leaving dark spots.

Some acne-fighting ingredients include, salicylic acid, sulphur, benzoyl peroxide and niacinamide. 

Some ingredients also tackle acne and hyperpigmentation concurrently, such as retinoids and azelaic acid.

caption:
London part 1 😎🇬🇧 here’s the background story:

I did an internship in London for a few months when I was 22. I was a broke student and coming from Korea, everything was so expensive. The tube fair was too much so I would walk the bridge to commute to save money. I depended on a lot of £1 baguette to fill me up. I had 4 flat mates and 1 roommate—the one I was sharing the room with. I had my architecture & design portfolio with me and walked around south bank knocking on doors on random architecture & interior design studios to get a job (although no one took me seriously). This was London for me.

Fast forward to now, 13 years later, here on the same ground on a much different perspective. I’m working with the most caring @cultbeauty team to open a pop-up Café @kravebeauty in the middle of soho. Then meeting all of you who watched my videos to clear your skin or those who have been supporting the brand for years in person was such a surreal experience. My heart is full and my stomach is fuller. Also, to be able to gather people who I wanted to meet in person, at @spring_ldn at the most iconic @thesomersethouse was definitely a memory I’ll never forget. 

Now with @neilyeoh ‘s family members in London, I feel like my London experience has been added so much color to it. The 22 yo me would never had imagined this was going to be my London experience in 35. That’s all to say—ily London, you’ll always have a special place in my heart 🩵🌴🇬🇧

🇰🇷i freaking love that #kpop, #kbeauty, and #koreanfood is becoming more internationally recognized. it makes me proud as a korean. but you know what makes me the most proud? The ✨K-trash system✨😂

✅ Korea composts organic waste up to… 100% but it used to be 2.6% in 1996. So the U.S. has hope too! 

✅ Korea has pay-as-you-trash system. The more you trash, the more you pay. 

😓 Just because the US residents don’t ‘pay’ when throwing away something, that doesn’t mean they’re not paying. US spends nearly 200 billion in waste management a year and it comes from tax payer

✅ Korea’s recycling rate is at 58% that ranks us 2nd in the world after #Singapore. Koreans know how to sort their own recyclables into hard plastic, soft plastic, clear plastic, colored plastic, etc. 

📝Korea is not perfect. And no country or government is. But korea shows how effective the impact can be when regulations are implemented.

I was literally wearing my shades at my desk this morning haha 😎

When indoors, the amount of sky visible from your location correlates with your exposure to UVA rays, which can penetrate window glass. These rays contribute significantly to skin aging and the risk of skin cancers. So, the more sky you see, the greater your potential exposure. 
Therefore, applying a broad-spectrum sunscreen with an SPF of at least 30 is crucial, even indoors. This level of SPF protects against both UVA and UVB rays. Regular application is advised if you’re seated near windows with a clear view of the sky, enhancing your skin’s defense against these harmful effects. 

Bottom line… Id you’re a desk worker like me, take a minute to assess your situation and make your own informed decision 😊 

#sunprotection #spf #skincareroutine #skinhealth #dermatology

AD | 🗑️Did you know Dove’s sachets are unrecyclable? Beauty has a #packaging crisis and the single-use #plasticsachets are a major culprit. And at the center of it, there’s Unilever, the parent company of @Dove, which is the biggest corporate seller of plastic sachets in the world. (Source: Greenpeace International Analysis)

🚮These tiny plastic sachets are nearly impossible to recycle and it’s polluting communities such as India, Indonesia, and the Philippines.

📈The company first promised to tackle the problem of sachet waste in 2010. Instead, it went on to produce an estimated 475 billion over the next decade, steadily increasing sachet production by approximately 1-2 billion items a year. (Source: Greenpeace International Analysis)

🕊️And Dove pumps out 1,700 unrecyclable sachets every second which results in generating billions of units of single-use plastic each year.

🚰Solution? Reuse and refill: Dove and Unilever have the reach and definitely the resources, I mean they’re a $60-billion-dollar company. Imagine how amazing Unilever’s reuse and refill stores would be worldwide. It’s not only cheaper for customers but more environmentally friendly.

😓But instead, they’re choosing to pump out 1,700 unrecyclable sachets every second.

📩Please join me in signing the @Greenpeaceuk open letter demanding Dove to end their toxic sachet sales now. Link in bio

#DoveRealHarm #LetsChangeDove #CleanBeauty

𝗦𝗢𝗔𝗣𝗦 𝗩𝗦 𝗕𝗘𝗔𝗨𝗧𝗬 𝗕𝗔𝗥𝗦// 𝗜 𝗱𝗼𝗻’𝘁 𝗹𝗶𝗸𝗲 𝘀𝗼𝗮𝗽𝘀 𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗳𝗮𝗰𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝘀 𝘄𝗵𝘆. ⁣
⁣
👉🏾Soaps and beauty bars both cleanse, but differ in ingredients and formulation.⁣
👉🏽𝗦𝗼𝗮𝗽𝘀 are made from 𝗻𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗳𝗮𝘁𝘀 𝗼𝗿 𝗼𝗶𝗹𝘀 combined with an alkali, typically 𝘀𝗼𝗱𝗶𝘂𝗺 𝗵𝘆𝗱𝗿𝗼𝘅𝗶𝗱𝗲 (𝗹𝘆𝗲), in a process called saponification.⁣
👉🏿𝗦𝗼𝗮𝗽𝘀 have a 𝗵𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗽𝗛 (around 9-10) compared to the skin’s 𝗻𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗽𝗛 (𝗮𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱 5.5), which can 𝗱𝗶𝘀𝗿𝘂𝗽𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝗸𝗶𝗻’𝘀 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗯𝗮𝗿𝗿𝗶er, leading to dryness‼️, irritation‼️and damage‼️⁣
⁣
👉🏽𝗕𝗲𝗮𝘂𝘁𝘆 𝗕𝗮𝗿𝘀 (𝗦𝘆𝗻𝗱𝗲𝘁𝘀) are made from synthetic surfactants instead of traditional soap ingredients.⁣
👉🏾Syndets are formulated to match the pH of the skin more closely & have moisture locking ingredients making them gentler and less likely to cause issues with the skin’s protective barrier.⁣
⁣
⁣#skincare #skintips #dove 

Were you aware of this difference ?

BBQ season 😍

Lebanese-Indian Fusion BBQ Chicken Recipe

- 4 chicken thighs skin on 
- 3 tablespoons olive oil
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon plain yogurt
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 teaspoon ground cumin
- 1 teaspoon ground coriander
- 1 teaspoon ground turmeric
- 1 teaspoon paprika
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon ground black pepper
- 1 teaspoon sumac 
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon garam masala (Indian spice blend)
- 1 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley (for garnish)

1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, yogurt, and minced garlic. Add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, paprika, cinnamon, black pepper, sumac (if using), salt, and garam masala. Mix well to create a smooth marinade.

2. Place the chicken pieces  in the bowl, ensuring they are well-coated with the marinade. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, preferably overnight, to allow the flavours to penetrate the meat.

3. Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. If using a charcoal grill, make sure the coals are hot and have a light coating of ash.

4. Remove the chicken from the marinade, allowing any excess to drip off. Place the chicken breasts on the grill. Cook for about 6-7 minutes on each side or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (75°C) and the chicken is nicely charred.

5. Once cooked, remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. This helps the juices redistribute, making the chicken more tender and flavorful.

6. Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving. Pair with a side of grilled vegetables, a fresh salad, or warm pita bread.

#chickensalad #bbqchicken #couscous #bbq #salad

🛍️When you’re buying a beauty product, you do know what’s inside the formula from the ingredient list. However, what you don’t know is how the ingredients are farmed, harvested, and how the workers and stakeholders	are treated throughout the entire process, often involving child labor, deforestation, and ethical concerns.

🍌So let’s say a brand wants to use a banana extract for their product. And, there are multiple farms producing bananas. but instead of the brand going directly to the farms, it’s normal to go through a middle man that distributes the banana extract to the brand.

🤝This is basically the brand entrusting the distributor that they did the due diligence and vetted the source. However the downside is, the brand really doesn’t know how the banana extract is sourced, which land its used, and who harvested the crops.

🌳You know we have tamanu oil in our best selling KraveBeauty’s Great Barrier Relief. And we used to use a distributor to get tamanu oil from Madagascar. But one day, the distributor said they can’t get any tamanu oil from the original farm. It suddenly disappeared. 

🫶So that’s when we pressed reset and decided to work directly with a specific farm in Indonesia. The goal was to know exactly where our tamanu oil came from, how it’s farmed, harvested and to increase the wages of women producers in the region and largely, to contribute to the climate resilience as a whole.

🥹And NOW! we’re proud to finally present the fruit of this project. Our 100% cold-pressed, organic tamanu oil (limited edition) and by popular request, our JUMBO Great Barrier Relief that uses sustainably sourced tamanu oil in each bottle. 

💰This was a hella expensive process not gonna lie but a meaningful one to us. Please like this video and share this if you can. That would mean a lot to us and our tamanu farmers. 

🛒Link in bio to support this initiative and 5% of the sales will be reinvested in the tamanu oil agroforestry & increasing the wages of women producers in the region.

✨Lisbon…WOW. We were blown away by your beautiful cuisine and the availability of plant-based foods. As you can see, it ranged from gourmet to fast food and everything in between and we loved it! 

Highlight was finding a vegan version of the national delicacy Pasteis de Nata, which is eaten with a cheeky glass of port - delicious!

To answer the question why I went plant-based? Well, I read recently in the Women’s Health Initiative report that higher vegetable protein intake was associated with lower breast cancer incidence and lower risk of death after breast cancer. And the opposite was true for animal protein.

Having had breast cancer twice, this and other reports have convinced me to give up the animal protein part of my diet. In addition, the hormones fed to animals can affect our hormones, and both my cancers were hormone receptive. It’s a no brainer. 

I’m aiming to stack the health odds in my favour and hope this will help to discourage the cancer from coming back 🤞

I’m keen to stress here that I’m not trying to convince everyone to do the same. This is my journey, but I’m sharing in case there’s a woman in my situation reading this who doesn’t know the potential benefits. 

It’s also nice to feel that I am potentially lowering my overall environmental footprint! 

🇵🇹So, thank you, Lisbon, and all of your amazing restauranteurs and vendors - we loved you! A gigantic ‘nom’ to you all 😂

Any questions - ask away! 

Julie 🤍✨

✨Are you worried about chemicals in products? 

I am. 

I used to love Duty Free, the beautiful displays, gorgeous packaging (which costs more than the product!), the feeling of luxury, browsing for a treat at the end of my holiday. And, of course, I trusted the companies to make safe products. 

But at the airport yesterday, not one of the tens of brands, makeup and skincare, I looked at were free of my personal seven toxic non-negotiables
- Parabens
- Phthalates
- Pegs
- Quats 
- Undefined fragrance (fragrance ingredients not declared) 
- Petroleum 
- Phenoxyethanol 

There are others of concern but these are the main culprits - linked to endocrine disruption, nerve damage, skin irritation and contamination by dioxins and other toxic chemicals. We ingest them in lipstick, absorb them through our skin in makeup and breathe them in when we spray perfume. 

And the companies know. The research is all out there. 

It’s personal, of course, and not everyone is worried about this, but we load our bodies with so many chemicals daily, and research charities like @breastcancernow and @breastcanceruk mention the importance of reducing our exposure to help limit risks. 

Needless to say, I didn’t buy anything! 

Please check what you are using if you can. If you’d like more info on this, follow for info about my free Clean Beauty Guide which will be dropping soon! 

In the meantime, ask if you have any questions. If I know it, I’m happy to help. 
Julie x ✨🤍

✨I’m not posting to tell you to stop dyeing your hair. 

I’m posting to remind myself how proud I am for letting go of the me who: 
- was ashamed of my grey hair showing
- was duped by societal pressure & beauty companies for decades into spending a fortune dyeing it
- wasted precious months of my life in the hairdressers
- unwittingly had toxic industrial chemicals in contact with my scalp every six to eight weeks for over 30 years 
- was scared of showing who I really was underneath the dye. 
It’s a journey. 
It’s a release. 
It’s worth every second. 
It’s one of the top things I’ve achieved in my life. 
It makes my heart dance 😂
Woohooo! 

I’m here if you feel the same and want to ask how. 
DM or comment. 

Much silvery love to all my sisters, 
Julie ✨🤍

Expanding on an already great video!

1. The cause for the pigmentation needs to determined - this will help in directing what you do to manage the hyperpigmentation 
2. Putting a time frame on how long it takes to improve is tricky and could be misleading but I do feel that with consistent use of good science backed products, there can be significant fading of some types of pigmentation 
3. Using spf, a tyrosinase inhibitor (vitamin c serum etc), with an vitamin A derivative (retinol/retinol) are easy things you can do before heading towards prescription creams/treatments 
4. Ingredients like Niacinamide are totally underused when it comes to managing pigmentation and especially in those with oily skin, with low levels of inflammation in the skin. A lot of skincare products now have niacinamide in them and so there is not often a need to use a separate niacinamide- look for a spf/ moisturiser with niacinamide in it- this can be helpful

My biggest tip: If you find something is stinging /burning or causing redness on your skin, stop using it. No cosmetic product bought over the counter should burn or sting

I hope you found this useful, let me know if you have any questions ⬇️ 

Thank you @dr_ingky for a great video !

Masala Chicken & quinoa burgers with green chutney

- 500 grams ground chicken
- 1 cup cooked quinoa
- 1 small onion, finely chopped
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch ginger, minced
- 2 green chilies, finely chopped
- 2 tablespoons fresh coriander leaves, chopped
- 1 teaspoon ground cumin
- pinch of turmeric
- 1 teaspoon ground coriander
- 1 teaspoon garam masala
- 1 teaspoon red chili powder (adjust to taste)
- Salt to taste
- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup breadcrumbs (more if needed)

For the Burger:
- Burger buns
- Lettuce leaves
- Tomato slices
- Onion rings
- Cheese slices (optional)
- Mayonnaise
- Green chutney (or sauce of your choice)

 1. In a large bowl, combine ground chicken, cooked quinoa, chopped onion, minced garlic, minced ginger, green chilies, and fresh coriander.
 Add ground cumin, ground coriander, garam masala, red chilli powder, and salt. Add the egg and breadcrumbs. Mix well until all ingredients are combined. If the mixture is too wet, add more breadcrumbs. Form the mixture into patties of desired size. 

2. Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium heat and add a little oil.  Cook the chicken quinoa patties for about 5-7 minutes on each side, or until fully cooked through and golden brown.

3. Toast the burger buns lightly on the grill or in a toaster. Spread mayonnaise and chutney on the bun. Place a lettuce leaf, followed by the chicken quinoa patty, a slice of cheese (if using), tomato slices, and onion rings. Serve the masala chicken quinoa burger hot with your favourite sides like fries or a salad.

Enjoy your delicious and nutritious masala chicken quinoa burger!

#sunday #easycooking #homecooking #chickenburger #burger #masalaburgers #indianfood

The biggest take away from my recent Italy trip. Food is medicine here.

❤️‍🩹In the past 2 months, I had constant, literally daily stomach issues in the states. I had to rely on probiotics, antibiotics, and all sorts of meds which I brought to the trip and surprisingly never needed. My stomach feels so comfortable 🥹

🍅The foods not only more delicious but it makes you feel nourished. The ingredients are fresh and the produce is more nutritious. Man, the tomatoes here are extremely juicy and sweet. Makes me feel that all other tomatoes I had in my life were fake 😅

🍞 The bread in America is quite honestly loaded with preservatives and you can taste it. It’s one of the biggest difference I noticed when I moved to America. But yes, Italy also proved that American breads are tasteless 🥴

🍷They take their craft pretty seriously. Yes, we visited wineries, farms and cooking classes but 

☕️ B&B in Italy is literally, a bed & BREAKFAST and the hosts take this very seriously! We were in awe with the breakfast spread each morning. Also, the espressos and mokapot coffee you taste in the countryside is seriously the best. I miss this already.

🍕I knew the pastas are legit but the pizzas here are prob the only pizza I actually eat the crusts too. 

🍧 how many gelatos a day are accepatable?

3 days in la in a nutshell🌴

🌴tried the viral #erewhon smoothie for $18! ty @merong.eth 
🌴two rows of palm palm

🌴stayed out in malibu by myself until I freaked out by nature 
🌴finally met @janethechemist irl but felt like we already met for 3 years 
🌴also celebrating how gorgeous @bonitravo ‘s blush looked 
🌴in-n-out order: protein style cheese burger, added raw and grilled onion. extra well done fries 🍟 
🌴birria.. ramyun 
🌴gears
not me but maybe my future daughter who grew up in the states 😅 #ai #aiyearbook #epik

✨There are deep roots in society as well as the earth: roots that have generated strong ideas but aren’t always beneficial. 

For example - the moment we buy an ‘anti-aging’  cream to ‘make us younger’, we’ve bought into, and perpetuate, a financially profitable root of fear instilled in women for 100s of years - namely that Youth equals Worth. 

How to weaken the root? Firstly, know that you have worth at every age 🤍 

Secondly, the strength in the root is diminished every time you chose a product that doesn’t scare you into buying it with claims of regaining youth. Choose where you spend your money. 

And big business? Isn’t it time for you to change your language and stop shaming us for profit?

Thanks for reading - thoughts?
J 🤍✨

Gujarati khaata Mung recipe 

 Ingredients:

- 1 cup whole green moong beans
- 1 cup yogurt (preferably slightly sour)
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 teaspoon mustard seeds
- 3-4 curry leaves 
- 1 teaspoon cumin seeds
- 1 pinch asafoetida (hing)
- 1/4 teaspoon turmeric powder
- 1 teaspoon red chili powder
- 1 teaspoon coriander powder
- 1/2 teaspoon garam masala
- Salt to taste
- 2-3 green chilies, slit
- 1-inch piece of ginger, grated
- 2 tablespoons gram flour (besan)
- 1 tablespoon jaggery (optional, for a slight sweetness)
- Fresh coriander leaves, chopped for garnish

1. Wash the moong beans thoroughly and soak them in water for about 4-5 hours or overnight.
Drain the soaked moong beans and rinse them again.
In a pressure cooker, add the moong beans and enough water to cover them.
Cook for about 3-4 whistles or until the beans are soft but not mushy. Set aside.

2. In a bowl, whisk the yogurt until smooth. Add the gram flour to the yogurt and mix well to ensure there are no lumps. This will help in thickening the curry.
 Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt to the yogurt mixture. Mix well and set aside.

3.Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Then add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add the grated ginger and slit green chilies and curry leaves. Sauté for a minute. Lower the heat and gradually add the yogurt mixture to the pan, stirring continuously to prevent the yogurt from curdling.
Add the cooked moong beans along with the water they were cooked in. If necessary, add a bit more water to get a slightly thin consistency.
 Add jaggery (if using) and mix well.
 Let the mixture simmer on low heat for about 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
 Add garam masala and mix well.
 Turn off the heat and garnish with chopped fresh coriander leaves.

4. Serve hot with steamed rice or roti.

Enjoy!

I’m often asked what I’d say to my younger self… I’ve found it a tricky question to answer. Young Mia definitely had moments of low level anxiety, that she confused with failure, as if it was somehow her fault. There wasn’t the same awareness around anxiety back then. However, despite that, and even though adversity, unimaginable grief, struggle and pain would visit the young woman on the left, she did good! She built her own coping mechanisms, her resilience, her strength, and her resourcefulness. And if it wasn’t for the lessons young Mia learnt, the middle aged woman on the right perhaps wouldn’t be flourishing and blooming in her season with such gratitude… she may not be equally comfortable in vulnerability and softness as she is in her power. I’ve never felt more self aware, self assured or more content than I do today. This combined with a heightened compassionate sense of purpose have made my 50s, undoubtedly, some of the best years of my life! So I guess, with a warm comforting hug, I’d hold my younger self tenderly to my heart and with a gentle sway in my arms I’d softly whisper in her ear “Thank you Mia, thank you.”
.
.
What would you say to your younger self? Let me know in the comments below. 
.
.
.
.
Thanks to my friend @untillemonsrsweet for putting this thought in my mind this morning
.

✨ Ingredients lists are a nightmare to navigate if you’re keen to eliminate toxic chemicals, so I thought I’d share three ingredients to avoid in all personal care products, and why. 

Look carefully for these if you are at all worried about chemical load - the burden we are all adding to whenever we layer ourselves with products that contain harmful ingredients. Each of these is designated ‘safe’ at a certain percentage but no one knows what happens when they are combined. 

❌1. PEGs - anything that starts with ‘PEG’ is derived from petroleum (Polyethylene glycols). Depending on manufacturing processes, PEGs may be contaminated with measurable amounts of ethylene oxide and 1,4-dioxane which are highly toxic to humans and are known to have carcinogenic properties.PEGs and their derivatives are widely used in cosmetics as surfactants, cleansing agents, emulsifiers, skin conditioners, and humectants. PEGS are also not biodegradable. 

❌2. ‘Quats’ or Quaternary ammonium compounds, e.g. - Quaterniums 10, 15, 18 which are known human skin toxicants, allergens and possible eye irritants. 15 is also a formaldehyde-releasing preservative. Formaldehyde is often the culprit of skin irritation and allergic reactions and is carcinogenic. Benzalkonium chloride is another quat to be avoided for the same reasons. They are nicknamed ‘Asthmagens’ for their effect on the respiratory system. 

❌3. Parabens - Butyl, propyl, and ethyl parabens are preservatives that have been linked to hormone disruption. I include these again because I found a big brand -  Vaseline - using them in some products this week! You can also find PEGs there too - shameful as many people use these on babies and children. 

Any Qs, ask away - love you all for reading if you got this far!

Julie 🤍✨🤍✨

⁣
𝗙𝗲𝗿𝗿𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗻 𝗩𝗦 𝗜𝗿𝗼𝗻 Levels //⁣
⁣
𝗙𝗲𝗿𝗿𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗻 𝗹𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗹𝘀 not 𝗜𝗿𝗼𝗻 𝗹𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗹𝘀  are a better indicator of iron deficiency anemia because they reflect the body’s iron stores more accurately than simple blood iron levels. ⁣
⁣
Iron levels can fluctuate due to various factors, while ferritin provides a more stable representation of iron reserves in the body.⁣
⁣
If you are having symptoms of anemia, make sure an assessment of your ferritin level is included in your tests. ⁣

One thing I know now that I wish I knew in my 20’s was to not overcomplicate things. The simplest answer and the quietest message are often the most important ones to listen to. I literally follow this advice in every aspect of my life. When I’m creating a treatment plan for a patient for example. And it’s also so important when you’re learning who you are in different roles in your life, whether it’s at work or at home or in friendships. Heck, this even applies to skincare and your grocery cart. My point is don’t overthink things and trust your instincts and when you don’t know, know what you don’t know and find an expert who does. “I have a foreboding of an America when the United States is a service and information economy; when nearly all the manufacturing industries have slipped away to other countries; when awesome technological powers are in the hands of a very few, and no one representing the public interest can even grasp the issues; when the people have lost the ability to set their own agendas or knowledgeably question those in authority; when, clutching our crystals and nervously consulting our horoscopes, our critical faculties in decline, unable to distinguish between what feels good and what's true, we slide, almost without noticing, back into superstition and darkness...

The dumbing down of America is most evident in the slow decay of substantive content in the enormously influential media, the thirty second sound bites, now down to ten seconds or less, lowest common denominator programming, credulous presentations on pseudoscience and superstition, but especially a kind of celebration of ignorance.” — Carl Sagan (search Charlie Rose 1996 interview; caption is another statement from the same conversation, not subtitles to the video; lots of mind fuel in this interview)

I know you love food, and I do too 😋 but let’s talk about #foodwaste and #composting. The things that alarmed me when I moved to NYC from Korea was 1) the abundance of food and that 2) composting is not a norm. 

In the US alone, food waste or food loss generates the equivalent of 32.6 million cars’ worth of greenhouse gas emissions. and sadly, only about 6% of food waste gets composted. NYC tho as a NYC compost app and bins all throughout different boroughs, so definitely check it out. If your neighborhood doesn’t have a compost bin, check out your local community garden.  
Why I recommend you #compost your food scraps!

🌱First, it reduces the waste stream 
🌱Second, it cuts methane emissions from landfills. Again, food waste is one of the biggest contributors to greenhouse gas emissions.  
🌱Third, it Improves soil health providing more nutrient dense crops back to us

I hope you can join me in composting—the easiest way for us to be more mindful about what we leave behind ☺️

taggedUsers.full_name: New Day Originals by Finula Crowe

caption:
✨When Fin, owner/designer of @newdayoriginals told me that Perseverance was the name of the stunning dress she wanted to send, I was in! #gifted 

Apart from the fact that the dress:
✨is stunningly luxurious and soft
✨hand-printed
✨sustainable, vegan and ethically produced
✨has pockets (🤩)
✨comes with the beautiful tie belt 
✨can be dressed up, down any way you like, 
✨AND is 20% off (as is everything on the site until the end of the month with my code - The_Fiftyist - 20) 

Apart from all that, the name holds meaning for me and I said yes immediately because it felt like it was meant to be. Ever get that feeling? 

You see, ‘Persevere’ is the motto my grandmother and mum adopted - both of whom had breast cancer. All of us (and my aunt as well, who passed with it) have had this awful disease and have gone through the treatments and the mental and physical stress they cause. 

My nan - Annie - died when I was 6 weeks old, but she held me beforehand which I’m so thankful for. The treatments she struggled through then fed the knowledge for my treatments in 2001, and then in 2021. 

It’s a familial medical timeline of bravery and perseverance, and I’m deeply proud of her, my aunt and mum. My mum (thankfully alive!) also took part in drug trials during her treatment to make them better for everyone. 

So, to persevere. It’s a powerful word - enough to strengthen resolve and help me through, and it has, and still does. And now wearing this beautiful dress reminds me of that. 

Thank you, Nan, Connie, Mum 
and Fin xx 

Note: when I told Fin the story, she was the one who suggested I post it.

Thanks for reading, and please check out Fin’s gorgeous designs - they are truly lovely!

Julie ✨🤍

It’s that time of the year… when sunscreen misinformation and tanning enters the chat. So… a few things:

✔️ tanning is a modern invention popularized by #cocochanel and #josephinebaker — French women wanted to look like Josephine Baker (a Black woman) so much so that Baker sold one of the first tanning oils to have ‘la peau brune’ — aka ‘brown skin.’ People didn’t intentionally tan. Let’s let that one go. Some sun is fine, but let’s not pretend pale people are supposed to be tanning for health.

✔️ your level of risk is absolutely predicated on genetics… but a lot of the people promoting tanning/no sunscreen… are my Pantone. If your pale, your genetics have asked you to not be outside if you live near the equator.

✔️ lots of things outside of sunscreen that you can do! Protective clothing, shade, time of day to be outside. I love being outdoors and I love the sun. I just don’t raw dog it, I practice safe sun 😌

#sun #sunscreen #beauty #beautyhistory

Next time I’ll actually cook 🧑‍🍳 

Saw @algaecookingclub on @sophia_roe’s IG and when I saw biotech cooking oil I had to try.

Why? Plant based products are much better for the environment than say animals but it still requires industrial agriculture. To get the scale needed, you have to use a lot of land. Factor pricing and privilege and for me… biotech really is the future.

Can’t wait to cook more and explain the technology behind it (algae is a wild lil organism), but till then, it really does taste like butter 😂

AD | A little collagen 101 with my Elemis night routine:

First of all what is collagen? It is a protein which is naturally found in the skin (the dermis) and it is one of the fundamental building blocks for soft, plump and firm skin.

As we’re ageing gracefully it is something that begins to deplete naturally (from mid 20’s) also by other external factors  which tends to lead to the skin looking dull, dry 

Building and maintaining a good skincare routine can really help increase skin quality and protect the collagen we have. Avoiding harsh treatments such as over exfoliating, using sunscreen need and focusing on nourishing, replenishing the skin.

One of my favourites from Elemis has to be the iconic cleansing balm! Absolutely love the gorgeous buttery texture and how it removes makeup and dirt without stripping the skin. it also protects the skin’s natural collagen stores and maintain collagen synthesis 🎀
@elemis_uki

3 THINGS I DID TO TRANSFORM MY SKIN ⬇️
- I switched my focus from exfoliants to retinol. I was too scared to use retinol because of my sensitive skin. I still use exfoliants but not as often (retinol speeds up your skin cell turnover!)
- I skipped morning cleansing. Cleansing my skin 3 times a day was leaving my skin barrier inflammed dehydrated and dry. 
- I started hydrating mists. Fine lines (esp in your 20s!) are just dehydration. I mist AM throughout the day and PM. Game. Changer.

3 THINGS I DID THAT CHANGED MY SKIN ⬇️
👉🏼I stopped cleansing my skin in the morning. I was only cleansing every morning because I thought that was what I was “supposed” to do. Even if you have clog prone skin, this is a game changer, try it!
👉🏼 I stopped exfoliating everyday. I had this warped view that if I peeled and peeled away at my skin it would be clear and glowy. That’s not how it works. I still exfoliate but I don’t have a strict routine, I’ll just exfoliate as and when and it’s never more than 1 X a week. I still love a physical scrub for getting rid of fake tan in the hairline don’t @ me Iol
👉🏼 I started using retinol PROPERLY. Ie not just getting a prescription strength and lathering it on like a mask each day (always read the instruction kids or itl end in tears and an anti histamine whilst out on a 1st date - true story). Now I’ll apply a pea sized amount for the whole face 1 or 2 X a week in the PM. I’ll use the sandwich method if it’s a strong retinol. Remember when starting retinol, exfoliate less. Your skin is resurfacing at a faster rate so there isn’t a big need for exfoliating as there once was.
I moved back in with my mum age 26 and 7 years later it’s still the best decision I ever made! 🤣

It was Black Maternal Health week. Maternal mortality in the US is on the rise, and Black women are at a shockingly high risk. Maternal health, and Black maternal health should be a year round concern ❤️

(Also, chemical sunscreen is fine, but, if you’re worried, UPF clothing and hats because most mineral sunscreens in the US use boosters that are chemical sunscreen analogues placing them in the category more of ‘hybrids’ rather than ‘100% mineral’).

Scenes

Forget everything you thought you knew about radiant skin! ✨ As a dermatologist who’s consulted for top brands and helped countless people achieve glowing skin, I’m sharing 5 surprising (and science-backed!) facts YOU can use right now. Let’s get started!

📍1. Ceramide Levels are Key (PMID: 28180020): Ceramides are like mortar between skin cells, locking in moisture and preventing dullness. Choose cleansers that don’t strip ceramides and moisturizers that contain them.

📍2. Stress Takes a Toll (PMID: 23378680): Chronic stress triggers cortisol, which can break down collagen and lead to uneven skin tone. Prioritize stress-management techniques like meditation or yoga for a healthy glow.

📍3. Gentle Exfoliation is Your Friend (PMID: 30130032): Dead skin cells can make skin look rough and dull. Exfoliate 1-2 times a week with a gentle chemical exfoliant like AHAs or BHAs for a brighter complexion.

📍4. Beware of Inflammatory Foods (PMID: 27927232): Processed foods and excessive sugar can trigger inflammation, which can manifest as redness and uneven skin tone. Focus on a whole-foods diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and healthy fats.

📍5. Heat Triggers Hyperpigmentation (PMID: 17299357): Sun exposure is a well-known culprit, but did you know heat can also worsen dark spots? Avoid excessive hot showers, saunas, and be mindful of sun protection even on cloudy days!

#ScienceofRadiance #ToneDermatology #TheToneMethod #SkincareScience #RadiantSkin #SkinofColorDermatologist #GlowUp 

Join me here for more radiance tips ✨

Tips for getting through the peeling process:

Always follow the post treatment directions given after your peel ✨

• If there’s excess dead skin and you’re tempted to pick or play with it just snip it off with a curved scissor to avoid extra potential issues (hyperpigmentation/scarring)

• Always cleanse with a gentle hydrating cleanser and don’t rinse off with hot water 🫠

*Air dry or pat your skin dry. Last thing you want to do is snag any hanging bits of skin 

• Moisturise Moisturise Moisturise (if you’re not given a moisturiser you’re everyday one is fine as long as it doesn’t have any actives)
(I personally love adding an ointment at the end as my skin tends to feel super dry. This helps elevate the tightness

• Sunscreen. Full stop. 

• Patience - Your skin may darken and look wayward but trust the process in a week (+21 days) you’ll be looking super sweet 💓

My top 3 tips to prevent pilling⬇️
👉🏼Avoid more than one silicone product per routine and dont use too much of it. These are usually your blurring primers, and some moisturisers can be heavy in silicones if they give that kind of velvety smooth feel.
👉🏼Mist in between layers. This seems to work well for me, I think the fine mists help to absorb product into the skin rather than sit on surface.
👉🏼If you know you’re going to be applying a lot of makeup, keep your skincare routine steps to a minimum. Most SPFs can be moisturising enough to use as a moisturiser and SPF in one!

When you have to go to the #FrenchPharmacy because you forgot EVERYTHING at home 😂 (other than sunscreen). What I got…

✅ @larocheposay Retinol B3: Blend of #retinol and #niacinamide to gently nudge your collagen and cell turnover. I can probably use this every night because it’s so gentle. Tret I use 2-3x a week for context.
✅ @avene Cleanance—acne products and products that speed up cell turnover can go hand in hand — this has succinc acid which is anti inflammatory and anti microbial (for acne) but can also be helpful for sensitive skin that wants a glow. I can also use this every night because it’s so gentle. Typically I’d use @dr.idriss flash mask 1-2x a week.

So, while this is less dramatique skincare — I can be dramatic in how I use it which is OFTEN. 

CLARIFYING LAW ON AHAs…
law is you cannot have AHAs at ph of under 3.5 and a max concentration of 10%. Not sure anyone remembers the Sunday Riley good genes debacle where they had to pull the entire launch in 2018 because it had 7% lactic acid at a pH of 3. In 1997 the rule in Europe became nothing over 10% and nothing under a ph of 3.5. Found an opinion from the SCCNFP on AHAs in 2000 and based on cautionary principle, the recommendation was to have it be even lower at 2.5% lactic acid at or over a pH of 5 and glycolic should be max 4% at or over a pH of 3.8. Will make another video as I misspoke in this one on lactic acid!

HALO LASER, WAS IT WORTH IT? 

Look, it’s weird to care this much about my meat sack. I do enjoy how my skin looks and feels. Beauty practices can be comforting, but adhering to strict beauty standards can be distressing. Coming to terms with that duality.

Now… why it works. Halo laser is a blend of 2 wavelengths of lasers. It’s a hybrid fractional laser meaning it’s both surface and goes deeper.

Not the most intense laser out there, but spicier than say Clear + Brilliant and less spicy than a CO2 laser (très 🌶️

Halo, when done correctly, should be helping with collagen (meh on the claims tighter skin, more collagen = plump = appearance of tightness), fades hyperpigmentation, smooths skin, improves texture/scarring.

Now, the downtime. I’m glad I did it over the holiday. Almost 2 months in, the glow @drbhanusali told me would happen is here. Took about a week to fully recover with 3 days where I stayed in and didn’t go outside.

Some things to remember:

✅ I’m uncomfortable with how much I focused on my meat sack during recovery. This felt like a new level for me because I’ve never done a laser that required downtime before.

✅ if you’re more melanated go to an experienced provider. I don’t get hyperpigmentation from inflammation, I get red. And I got really red. If you’re prone to hyperpigmentation when you break out for example, you’re going to want to see if you can find a good provider (and you may have to go on hydroquinone so, just things to think about).

✅ some providers train for this on YouTube (seriously). Vet whoever is doing this on you, you don’t want to be the test subject.

✅ it’s expensive. $1500. Another thing that makes me uncomfortable but would I do it again, yes, in 2 years. 

✅ you’re a vampire now. No sun exposure!

✅ to me it’s not painful it’s uncomfortable—I have a high tolerance for pain though.

Any questions lmk in the comments! #laser #halolaser #skincare

Hi there, let me introduce you to the amazing Julie. I had the honor of being Julie’s empowerment coach when she found herself struggling to focus and feeling stuck.

We’ve all been there, right? But Julie, she decided to take the bull by the horns. She rolled up her sleeves, embraced accountability, and powered through it.

Julie and I had some incredible sessions together. She described my coaching style as “gentle and relaxed, yet incredibly perceptive.” Through our conversations, we dug deep, and I asked just the right questions that helped Julie turn her focus inward. It was like a light bulb moment – she could see the motivations behind her negative behaviors crystal clear. And with that clarity, she started making positive changes in her life.

Since our time together, Julie has achieved some fantastic things. She’s felt able to cut through the distractions and focus on completing her book, uses  planning techniques and software to help organise her time,  is writing for her website and feeling more confident to put herself out there on podcasts and in interviews - brilliant!

So, if nothing else let’s celebrate Julie @thefiftyist wins, and obviously follow her if you don’t already. 😉

Was so excited to get my hands on the new Melano CC Deep Day Care UV Milk SPF50 PA++++! A perfect compliment for their classic Vitamin C serums. We know by now that Vitamin C + Sunscreen in a morning routine gives more protection than sunscreen alone and are Summer essentials. This new sunscreen is truly an extension of the Vitamin Cs and contains 2 Vit C derivatives as well as Vit E. Also leaves a brightened, smooth, radiant skin finish with the same yuzu scent. 

My impressions so far - By any other standard, this is all-around decent enough with white cast potential on darker skin tones. But by Japanese sunscreen standards, this is nowhere as elegant as Skin Aqua (also Rohto), Biore, Anessa, Allie, Kao etc. Doesn’t have that weightless, even, silky application I’ve gotten used to. 

UV Filters: Octinoxate, Titanium Dioxide, Uvinul A plus, Tinosorb M, Tinosorb S

Been using @theordinary Multi-Peptide Eye Serum for a couple of months now, and can’t help comparing it to my beloved @Niod FECC. IMO they’re eye serums with different targets.

Multi-Peptide Eye Serum for the price point is still less expensive than anything else I find comparable and Deciem just does peptide formulas well. Perfect for those of us who’ve been using the former “Buffet” for the eye area. More hydrating than FECC, this is a great serum for preventation, puffiness, circles, and the starting signs of aging. 

FECC is more concentrated and exceptional at dealing with more acute signs of aging for skin that’s maturing. More immediate results in firmness esp on my upper lids and contains a legion of antioxidants. The texture is watery thin yet there’s some richness so I don’t need cream in the Summer.

Have you tried?

My skincare archives are going through a massive overhaul and I’m here getting all sentimental finding discontinued formulas that I once loved and have been irreplaceable. These are a few I can’t let go of and wanted to commemorate. I would still be using these today if they weren’t gone too soon. Many of you ‘early generation’ skinthusiasts will remember and were as gutted as I was when the brands/lines/formulas went “poof!” What do you miss? #iloveskincare#skincarelover

@theordinary Natural Moisturizing Factors + HA clearly would’ve been Goldilock’s choice of moisturizer, but for those of us who need more/less or who experience seasons, The Ordinary has 2 new ideal additions in their Natural Moisturizing Factors MF Collection. #Ad

I love that there is a significant distinction between these formulas, in that they can meet extreme skin needs but at the same time, I find myself enjoying both formulas and their textures depending on the time and place.
 
⁃ Natural Moisturizing Factors + Beta Glucan has a luscious gel texture. Feels glorious to apply - a refreshing hydration bomb. Finish is non-sticky & smooth. This has substantial lasting power and keeps my skin really plumped out. It’s oil-free, and contains Beta Glucan, Ceramides, and of course Natural Moisturizing Factors for all day hydration.
 
⁃ Natural Moisturizing Factors + Phytoceramides is my dream moisturizer for dry and sensitive skin days. It’s dense and intense in a way most moisturizers don’t dare, and will keep the skin surface well nourished with lipids. The emollience is comforting and this will give dull, dry skin a serious glow. Oily-combo skin folks beware!
 
#TheOrdinary #DrySkin #oilyskin #iloveskincare
